## Assistant (final_answer)

> I added the provider decision memo at [payments-provider-decision.md](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/docs/design/payments-provider-decision.md) and linked it from [INDEX.md](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/docs/INDEX.md).
>
> I also checked the actual Apple Pay flow surface area:
>
> - The only current end-to-end Apple Pay candidate flow is the web Account -> Stripe Checkout path in [AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx), backed by [CreateCheckoutSession in serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go) and [stripe.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/stripe.go).
> - The Apple app has a Billing screen in [BillingView.swift](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/app/Nanoclaw/Nanoclaw/Views/Billing/BillingView.swift), but it does not yet expose any Apple Pay-backed checkout or funding flow.
> - There is no current Apple Pay-specific automated test coverage. The targeted Go run found no billing/checkout-specific tests to execute, and the web UI build passed.
>
> Verification I ran:
> - `npm --prefix cmd/ncd/ui run build` passed
> - `go test ./cmd/ncd/billing ./cmd/ncd -run 'Test.*(Billing|Checkout|Portal|Account)'` passed, but with no billing package test matches
>
> So the current status is:
> - Strategy is now documented: Stripe-first billing, optional provider-backed x402 funding
> - Current shipped Apple Pay path is effectively just the web Stripe checkout path
> - The next practical step to “make sure end to end Apple Pay flow(s) work” is to define and test that web Stripe checkout path explicitly, then decide whether to add an Apple-app flow or an x402 funding flow next
>
> I recommend the next slice be one of these:
>
> 1. Add an explicit checkout flow verification plan and small integration tests for the web Stripe path.
> 2. Add a real Apple-app billing entry point if you want Apple Pay on Apple surfaces.
> 3. Add UI for the new x402 funding abstraction, still provider-agnostic, if you want to prepare for Coinbase or Stripe Crypto Onramp later.
>
> If you want, I can do the next artifact now: a concrete end-to-end Apple Pay verification plan for the current Stripe web checkout flow, including exact acceptance criteria and the code changes needed to make it testable.

## User

> yes lay out that plan

## Assistant (final_answer)

> **Goal**
>
> Verify the only current Apple Pay-capable path that plausibly exists today:
>
> - web `Account` -> `Upgrade Plan` -> Stripe Checkout -> success/cancel return
>
> Not in scope yet:
> - Apple app Apple Pay flow
> - x402 funding/provider onramp flow
>
> **Current Reality**
>
> Relevant code:
> - web entry: [AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx)
> - client RPC call: [client.ts](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/client.ts)
> - server checkout RPC: [serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go)
> - Stripe session creation: [stripe.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/stripe.go)
>
> Important caveat:
> - whether Apple Pay actually appears is partly a Stripe/dashboard/domain configuration issue, not just app code
>
> **Verification Plan**
>
> 1. Define acceptance criteria
>
> A flow is “working” if:
> - authenticated web user can click `Upgrade Plan`
> - server returns a valid Stripe Checkout URL
> - browser is redirected successfully
> - Stripe Checkout loads without server-side billing errors
> - on an Apple Pay-capable device/browser, Apple Pay is presented when Stripe and domain configuration allow it
> - success and cancel URLs return user to Nanoclaw without broken state
> - `Manage Billing` opens Stripe billing portal successfully
>
> 2. Add backend test coverage for session creation behavior
>
> Add focused tests around:
> - `CreateCheckoutSession` returns `CodeUnauthenticated` without auth
> - returns `CodeUnavailable` when billing is not configured
> - uses default price when request price is empty
> - returns checkout URL on success
> - `CreateBillingPortalSession` returns portal URL on success
>
> Best target:
> - new or expanded tests around [serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go)
>
> 3. Add a narrow abstraction seam for Stripe session creation
>
> Right now the handler reaches directly into Stripe service creation behavior. For reliable tests, introduce a small interface around:
> - `CreateCheckoutSession`
> - `CreatePortalSession`
> - `CreateCustomer`
> - `ListPaymentMethods`
>
> That lets tests verify handler semantics without real Stripe API calls.
>
> 4. Add frontend verification for Account billing actions
>
> At minimum:
> - test clicking `Upgrade Plan` calls `createCheckoutSession`
> - test loading state text appears
> - test browser redirect is attempted with returned URL
> - test `Manage Billing` calls `createBillingPortalSession`
> - test error toast path when RPC fails
>
> Target:
> - web tests around [AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx)
>
> 5. Add explicit deployment checklist for Apple Pay eligibility
>
> This is essential because code alone is insufficient.
>
> Checklist:
> - Stripe account has Apple Pay enabled for the relevant payment method configuration
> - Checkout mode and product type actually support Apple Pay in the deployed configuration
> - production domain is registered/verified as required by Stripe/Apple
> - test device is Safari on macOS/iOS with Wallet configured
> - region/browser/device combination is one Stripe supports
>
> 6. Run manual end-to-end validation
>
> Manual test matrix:
> - desktop Safari with Wallet configured
> - iPhone Safari with Wallet configured
> - non-Apple browser/device to confirm graceful fallback
>
> For each:
> - sign in
> - open Account
> - click `Upgrade Plan`
> - confirm redirect
> - confirm Checkout loads
> - confirm Apple Pay button presence or expected fallback
> - test cancel return
> - test success return in test mode if possible
> - test `Manage Billing`
>
> 7. Record outcomes in a conformance-style matrix
>
> For each RPC and surface:
> - happy path
> - unauthenticated
> - billing disabled
> - provider error
> - redirect success
> - cancel return
> - success return
> - Apple Pay available
> - Apple Pay unavailable fallback
>
> **Recommended Implementation Order**
>
> 1. Add backend tests and Stripe interface seam
> 2. Add small frontend tests for Account actions
>
> [truncated]

```text
+interface CheckoutSessionCreator {

+        successUrl: string;
+        cancelUrl: string;
+    }): Promise<{ checkoutUrl: string }>;
+}
+
+interface BillingPortalSessionCreator {
+    (request: {
+        returnUrl: string;
+    }): Promise<{ portalUrl: string }>;
+}
+
+interface CheckoutActionDeps {
+    origin: string;
+    createCheckoutSession: CheckoutSessionCreator;
+    setBusy: (busy: boolean) => void;
+    redirect: (url: string) => void;
+    notifyError: (message: string) => void;
+}
+
+interface BillingPortalActionDeps {
+    origin: string;
+    createBillingPortalSession: BillingPortalSessionCreator;
+    setBusy: (busy: boolean) => void;
+    redirect: (url: string) => void;
+    notifyError: (message: string) => void;
+}
+
+function accountBillingReturnUrl(origin: string): string {
+    return origin + "/#/app/account";
+}
+
+export async function startCheckoutAction(deps: CheckoutActionDeps): Promise<void> {
+    try {
+        deps.setBusy(true);
+        const res = await deps.createCheckoutSession({
+            priceId: "",
+            successUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
+            cancelUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
+        });
+        deps.redirect(res.checkoutUrl);
+    } catch (err: any) {
+        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
+        deps.notifyError("Checkout unavailable: " + message);
+        deps.setBusy(false);
+    }
+}
+
+export async function openBillingPortalAction(deps: BillingPortalActionDeps): Promise<void> {
+    try {
+        deps.setBusy(true);
+        const res = await deps.createBillingPortalSession({
+            returnUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
+        });
+        deps.redirect(res.portalUrl);
+    } catch (err: any) {
+        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
+        deps.notifyError("Billing portal unavailable: " + message);
```

```text
const bundlePath = join(tempDir, "account-view.cjs");

buildSync({
    entryPoints: ["/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx"],

const { startCheckoutAction, openBillingPortalAction } = await import(pathToFileURL(bundlePath).href);

after(() => {
    rmSync(tempDir, { recursive: true, force: true });

test("startCheckoutAction sends the checkout RPC, flips loading state, and redirects", async () => {
    const calls = [];
    await startCheckoutAction({
        origin: sandbox.origin,
        createCheckoutSession: async (request) => {
            calls.push(["rpc", request]);
            return { checkoutUrl: "https://checkout.example/session" };
        },
        setBusy: (busy) => {
            calls.push(["busy", busy]);
        },
        redirect: (url) => {
            calls.push(["redirect", url]);
        },
        notifyError: (message) => {
            calls.push(["error", message]);
        },
    });

    assert.deepEqual(calls, [
        ["busy", true],
        ["rpc", {
            priceId: "",
            successUrl: "https://nanoclaw.test/#/app/account",
            cancelUrl: "https://nanoclaw.test/#/app/account",
        }],
        ["redirect", "https://checkout.example/session"],
    ]);
});
```

```text
 func newX402Server(store db.X402Store, chainClient *chain.Client, treasuryAddr string, chainID uint64) *x402Server {
+	networkName := "base-sepolia"
+	if chainID == chain.BaseMainnetChainID {
+		networkName = "base"

@@ -375,6 +378,105 @@ func (s *x402Server) ListPaymentChannels(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Reque

+func (s *x402Server) ListFundingProviders(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdbillingx402v1.ListFundingProvidersRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdbillingx402v1.ListFundingProvidersResponse], error) {
+	_ = ctx
+	_ = req
+	var providers []*ncdbillingx402v1.FundingProvider
+	if coinbaseFundingEnabled() {
+		providers = append(providers, &ncdbillingx402v1.FundingProvider{
+			Key:            "coinbase_onramp",
+			DisplayName:    "Coinbase Onramp",
+			PaymentMethods: []ncdbillingx402v1.FundingPaymentMethod{ncdbillingx402v1.FundingPaymentMethod_FUNDING_PAYMENT_METHOD_APPLE_PAY, ncdbillingx402v1.FundingPaymentMethod_FUNDING_PAYMENT_METHOD_DEBIT_CARD, ncdbillingx402v1.FundingPaymentMethod_FUNDING_PAYMENT_METHOD_COINBASE_BALANCE},
+			Mode:           ncdbillingx402v1.FundingProviderMode_FUNDING_PROVIDER_MODE_HOSTED,
+			Asset:          "usdc",
+			Network:        s.networkName,
+			Active:         true,
+		})
+	}
+	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdbillingx402v1.ListFundingProvidersResponse{Providers: providers}), nil
+}
+
+func (s *x402Server) CreateFundingSession(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdbillingx402v1.CreateFundingSessionRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdbillingx402v1.CreateFundingSessionResponse], error) {
+	if req.Msg.Payer == nil {
+		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("payer required"))
+	}
+	if strings.TrimSpace(req.Msg.ProviderKey) == "" {
+		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("provider_key required"))
+	}
+	if req.Msg.ProviderKey != "coinbase_onramp" {
+		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("unsupported funding provider"))
+	}
+	if !coinbaseFundingEnabled() {
+		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("coinbase onramp not configured"))
+	}
+	id, err := newX402ID()
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, fmt.Errorf("generate funding session id: %w", err))
+	}
+	chainID := s.chainID
+	if req.Msg.ChainId != 0 {
+		chainID = req.Msg.ChainId
+	}
```

```text
	s.t.Helper()

}

func (s *stubBillingService) CreateCheckoutSession(customerID, successURL, cancelURL, priceID string) (string, error) {
	s.createCheckoutSessionCalls = append(s.createCheckoutSessionCalls, createCheckoutSessionCall{
		customerID: customerID,
		successURL: successURL,
		cancelURL:  cancelURL,
		priceID:    priceID,
	})
	return s.checkoutURL, nil
}

func (s *stubBillingService) CreatePortalSession(customerID, returnURL string) (string, error) {
	s.createPortalSessionCalls = append(s.createPortalSessionCalls, createPortalSessionCall{

		returnURL:  returnURL,
	})
	return s.portalURL, nil
}

	s.t.Helper()
	s.t.Fatalf("unexpected GetCustomer call: %s", id)
	return nil, nil
}

func (s *stubBillingService) ListPaymentMethods(customerID string) ([]*stripe.PaymentMethod, error) {
	s.t.Helper()
	s.t.Fatalf("unexpected ListPaymentMethods call: %s", customerID)

}

func testBillingAuthContext(t *testing.T, customerID string) context.Context {

	t.Parallel()

	srv := &nanoclawServer{}

	_, err := srv.CreateCheckoutSession(context.Background(), connect.NewRequest(&ncdv1.CreateCheckoutSessionRequest{}))

	}
	if connect.CodeOf(err) != connect.CodeUnauthenticated {
		t.Fatalf("code = %v, want %v", connect.CodeOf(err), connect.CodeUnauthenticated)

	}
	if connect.CodeOf(err) != connect.CodeUnavailable {

	}
}

	}

	}
	call := billing.createCheckoutSessionCalls[0]
	if call.priceID != "price_default" {
		t.Fatalf("price id = %q, want %q", call.priceID, "price_default")
	}
}
```

```text
---
title: Stripe Web Apple Pay Conformance
status: current
last_reviewed: 2026-04-10
---

# Stripe Web Apple Pay Conformance

This document covers the current web billing path where Nanoclaw sends an authenticated user from the Account screen to Stripe Checkout.

Relevant code:

- [cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx)
- [cmd/ncd/serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go)
- [cmd/ncd/billing/stripe.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/stripe.go)

## Scope

In scope:

- web account billing
- Stripe Checkout session creation
- billing portal session creation
- Apple Pay eligibility within Stripe Checkout when Stripe, browser, device, and domain configuration allow it

Out of scope:

- the Apple app billing screen
- x402 funding sessions
- Coinbase or Stripe Crypto Onramp

## Acceptance Criteria

1. An authenticated user can open the Account billing screen and request an upgrade.
2. Nanoclaw creates a Stripe Checkout session and returns a usable redirect URL.
3. The Checkout page loads successfully and allows completion of the purchase.
4. On an Apple Pay-capable Safari flow with the required Stripe configuration, Apple Pay is offered and can complete payment.
5. A cancel path returns the user to Nanoclaw without breaking session state.
6. A success path returns the user to Nanoclaw and the UI can refresh billing state.
7. `Manage Billing` opens the Stripe billing portal successfully.
8. Non-Apple browsers and devices degrade gracefully when Apple Pay is unavailable.
9. Server-side errors are surfaced in the UI and do not leave the user on a blank or ambiguous state.

## Deployment Checklist

1. Stripe products, prices, and customer portal settings are configured for the target plan.
2. Apple Pay is enabled in the relevant Stripe payment method configuration.
3. The production billing domain is registered and verified with Stripe and Apple, if required by the Stripe configuration.
4. The Checkout flow uses the correct live-mode API keys and price IDs.
5. Success and cancel return URLs are valid for the deployed domain.
6. Billing endpoints are served over HTTPS.
7. Test devices are prepared:
   - macOS Safari with a Wallet-configured Apple Pay account
   - iPhone Safari with a Wallet-configured Apple Pay account
   - at least one non-Apple browser for fallback validation
8. Logging is available for checkout and portal session creation failures.

## Manual Test Matrix

| Surface | Preconditions | Steps | Expected result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| macOS Safari | Signed in; Apple Pay configured in Wallet; Stripe Apple Pay enabled | Open Account -> click `Upgrade Plan` | Redirect to Stripe Checkout; Apple Pay is offered when eligible |
| iPhone Safari | Signed in; Apple Pay configured in Wallet; Stripe Apple Pay enabled | Open Account -> click `Upgrade Plan` | Redirect to Stripe Checkout; Apple Pay is offered when eligible |
| Non-Apple browser | Signed in; Apple Pay unavailable | Open Account -> click `Upgrade Plan` | Checkout still opens; payment fallback is available; no client crash |
| Any supported browser | Signed in | Open Account -> click `Manage Billing` | Stripe billing portal opens successfully |
| Any supported browser | Stripe returns an error | Open Account -> click `Upgrade Plan` | UI shows a useful error and remains usable |

## Conformance Matrix

| Flow | Conformance check | Pass condition |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `CreateCheckoutSession` | Auth, billing config, price mapping, redirect URL | Returns a valid Checkout session URL for an authenticated user |
| `CreateBillingPortalSession` | Auth, customer linkage, portal config | Returns a valid portal URL for an authenticated user |

[truncated]
```

## Assistant (final_answer)

> Implemented the current Stripe web Apple Pay verification slice.
>
> On the backend, [serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go) now uses a small `billingService` interface so checkout and portal handlers can be tested without real Stripe calls, and [serve_billing_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve_billing_test.go) adds focused coverage for unauthenticated access, billing unavailable, default price selection, checkout URL return, and billing portal URL return.
>
> On the frontend, [AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx) extracts the checkout and billing-portal actions into testable helpers, and [account-billing-actions.test.mjs](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/test/account-billing-actions.test.mjs) covers the success and error paths for both `Upgrade Plan` and `Manage Billing`. I also added the manual verification/conformance doc at [stripe-web-apple-pay-conformance.md](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/docs/design/stripe-web-apple-pay-conformance.md), plus the earlier provider decision memo at [payments-provider-decision.md](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/docs/design/payments-provider-decision.md).
>
> Verification run:
> - `go test ./cmd/ncd -run 'TestCreate(CheckoutSession|BillingPortalSession)'`
> - `node --test cmd/ncd/ui/test/account-billing-actions.test.mjs`
> - `npm --prefix cmd/ncd/ui run build`
>
> What is still not proven by automation is actual Apple Pay presentation inside Stripe Checkout on Safari/iPhone. That still requires the manual checklist in [stripe-web-apple-pay-conformance.md](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/docs/design/stripe-web-apple-pay-conformance.md): real Stripe config, verified domain, Safari, and Wallet-enabled devices.

```text
import { h, ComponentChildren } from "preact";
import { useState, useEffect } from "preact/hooks";

export type SurfaceId = "app" | "consumer" | "ops";

type SurfaceSpec = {
    id: SurfaceId;
    label: string;
    defaultPath: string;
};

export const SURFACE_SPECS: SurfaceSpec[] = [
    { id: "app", label: "Personal", defaultPath: "/app" },
    { id: "consumer", label: "Consumer", defaultPath: "/consumer" },
    { id: "ops", label: "Ops", defaultPath: "/ops/admin/testflight" },
];

const SURFACE_SET = new Set<SurfaceId>(SURFACE_SPECS.map(s => s.id));
export type LegalRouteId = "privacy" | "tos";
const LEGAL_ROUTE_PARTS: LegalRouteId[] = ["privacy", "tos"];
const LEGAL_ROUTE_SET = new Set<LegalRouteId>(LEGAL_ROUTE_PARTS);

export type PublicRouteId = "early-access";
const PUBLIC_ROUTE_SET = new Set<PublicRouteId>(["early-access"]);

export function surfaceDefaultPath(surface: SurfaceId): string {
    return SURFACE_SPECS.find(s => s.id === surface)?.defaultPath || "/app";
}

interface Route {
    path: string;
    parts: string[];
    surface: SurfaceId;
    legal?: LegalRouteId | null;
    publicRoute?: PublicRouteId | null;
    docsSlug?: string | null;
}

function parseParts(input: string): string[] {
    return input.split("/").filter(Boolean);
}

function normalizeAppParts(rest: string[]): string[] {
    if (rest.length === 0) return ["app"];
    if (rest[0] === "agents") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "agents", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "agents"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "sessions") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "sessions", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "sessions"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "audit") return ["app", "audit"];
    if (rest[0] === "roles") return ["app", "roles"];
    if (rest[0] === "enclaves") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "enclaves", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "enclaves"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "secrets") return ["app", "secrets"];
    if (rest[0] === "network") return ["app", "network"];
    if (rest[0] === "terminals") return ["app", "terminals"];
    if (rest[0] === "terminal") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "terminal", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "terminals"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "approvals") return ["app", "approvals"];
    if (rest[0] === "account") return ["app", "account"];
    if (rest[0] === "skills") return ["app", "skills"];
    if (rest[0] === "templates") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "templates", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "templates"];

    if (rest[0] === "workflows") {
        if (rest.length > 2) return ["app", "workflows", rest[1], rest[2]];
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "workflows", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "workflows"];
    }
    return ["app"];
}

function normalizeConsumerParts(rest: string[]): string[] {
    if (rest.length === 0) return ["consumer"];
    if (rest[0] === "network") return ["consumer", "network"];
    if (rest[0] === "enclaves") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["consumer", "enclaves", rest[1]];
        return ["consumer", "enclaves"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "approvals") return ["consumer", "approvals"];
    if (rest[0] === "account") return ["consumer", "account"];
    return ["consumer"];
}

function normalizeOpsParts(rest: string[]): string[] {
    if (rest.length === 0) return ["ops", "admin", "testflight"];

    if (rest[0] === "admin") return ["ops", "admin", normalizeOpsAdminSection(rest[1])];
```

```text
    dev: boolean;

const APPLE_JS_SRC = "https://appleid.cdn-apple.com/appleauth/static/jsapi/appleid/1/en_US/appleid.auth.js";

export function LoginView() {
    const [authStatus, setAuthStatus] = useState<AuthStatus | null>(null);
    const [error, setError] = useState("");

    const beginGoogleLogin = () => {
        if (authStatus && !authStatus.google) {
            setError("Google OAuth not configured. Set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, or use an API key.");
            return;
        }

        setError("");
        const width = 520;
        const height = 720;
        const left = Math.max(0, Math.floor((window.screen.width - width) / 2));
        const top = Math.max(0, Math.floor((window.screen.height - height) / 2));
        const popup = window.open(
            "/auth/google/login?popup=1",
            "ncd_google_login",
            `width=${width},height=${height},left=${left},top=${top},resizable=yes,scrollbars=yes`
        );
        if (!popup) {
            window.location.href = "/auth/google/login";
            return;
        }

        let done = false;
        const cleanup = () => {
            window.removeEventListener("message", onMessage as EventListener);
            clearInterval(watchClosed);
        };
        const onMessage = (event: MessageEvent) => {
            if (event.origin !== window.location.origin) return;
            if (!event.data || event.data.type !== "ncd-auth") return;
            done = true;
            cleanup();
            if (event.data.status === "ok") {
                window.location.reload();
                return;
            }
            setError("Google sign-in failed.");
        };
        window.addEventListener("message", onMessage as EventListener);

        const watchClosed = window.setInterval(() => {
            if (popup.closed) {
                clearInterval(watchClosed);
                window.removeEventListener("message", onMessage as EventListener);
                if (!done) {
                    setError("Google sign-in popup closed before completion.");
                }
            }
        }, 400);
    };

    const loadAppleSDK = async (): Promise<any> => {
        const existing = document.querySelector<HTMLScriptElement>(`script[src="${APPLE_JS_SRC}"]`);
        if (!existing) {
            await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
                const script = document.createElement("script");
                script.src = APPLE_JS_SRC;
                script.async = true;
                script.onload = () => resolve();
                script.onerror = () => reject(new Error("failed to load apple sign-in sdk"));
                document.head.appendChild(script);
            });
        }

        const appleID = (window as any).AppleID;
        if (!appleID?.auth) {
            throw new Error("apple sign-in sdk unavailable");
        }
        return appleID;
    };

    const beginAppleLogin = async () => {
        if (!authStatus?.apple) {
            setError("Apple sign-in is not configured.");
            return;
        }
        if (!authStatus.apple_client_id) {
            setError("Apple sign-in is missing apple client ID configuration.");
            return;
        }
```

```text
	allocCtx, allocCancel := chromedp.NewExecAllocator(context.Background(), opts...)
	t.Cleanup(allocCancel)

	browserCtx, browserCancel := chromedp.NewContext(allocCtx)

	ctx, timeoutCancel := context.WithTimeout(browserCtx, timeout)
	cancel := func() {
		timeoutCancel()
		browserCancel()
	}
	t.Cleanup(cancel)
	return ctx, cancel
}

// devLogin navigates to the dev login endpoint and waits for the app shell to
// appear. All tests that require authentication call this first.
func devLogin(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T) {
	t.Helper()
	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/auth/dev/login"),
		// After dev login the server redirects to the SPA.
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/app"),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(`nav, [data-testid="nav"]`, chromedp.ByQuery),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("dev login: %v", err)
	}
}

// navTo navigates to a path under cdpURL and waits for a CSS selector.
func navTo(ctx context.Context, path, waitSel string) chromedp.Action {
	return chromedp.Tasks{
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL + path),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(waitSel, chromedp.ByQuery),
	}
}

// clickAndWait clicks a selector, then waits for another to appear.
func clickAndWait(clickSel, waitSel string) chromedp.Action {
	return chromedp.Tasks{
		chromedp.Click(clickSel, chromedp.ByQuery),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(waitSel, chromedp.ByQuery),
	}
}

// getText returns the trimmed inner text of a selector.
func getText(ctx context.Context, sel string) (string, error) {
	var text string
	err := chromedp.Run(ctx, chromedp.Text(sel, &text, chromedp.ByQuery))
	return strings.TrimSpace(text), err
}

func intMax(a, b int) int {
	if a > b {
		return a
	}
	return b
}

func captureAndValidateScreenshot(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T, name string) {
	t.Helper()
```

```text
import { h, ComponentChildren } from "preact";
import { useState, useEffect } from "preact/hooks";

export type SurfaceId = "app" | "consumer" | "ops";

type SurfaceSpec = {
    id: SurfaceId;
    label: string;
    defaultPath: string;
};

export const SURFACE_SPECS: SurfaceSpec[] = [
    { id: "app", label: "Personal", defaultPath: "/app" },
    { id: "consumer", label: "Consumer", defaultPath: "/consumer" },
    { id: "ops", label: "Ops", defaultPath: "/ops/admin/testflight" },
];

const SURFACE_SET = new Set<SurfaceId>(SURFACE_SPECS.map(s => s.id));
export type LegalRouteId = "privacy" | "tos";
const LEGAL_ROUTE_PARTS: LegalRouteId[] = ["privacy", "tos"];
const LEGAL_ROUTE_SET = new Set<LegalRouteId>(LEGAL_ROUTE_PARTS);

export type PublicRouteId = "early-access";
const PUBLIC_ROUTE_SET = new Set<PublicRouteId>(["early-access"]);

export function surfaceDefaultPath(surface: SurfaceId): string {
    return SURFACE_SPECS.find(s => s.id === surface)?.defaultPath || "/app";
}

interface Route {
    path: string;
    parts: string[];
    surface: SurfaceId;
    legal?: LegalRouteId | null;
    publicRoute?: PublicRouteId | null;
    docsSlug?: string | null;
}

function parseParts(input: string): string[] {
    return input.split("/").filter(Boolean);
}

function normalizeAppParts(rest: string[]): string[] {
    if (rest.length === 0) return ["app"];
    if (rest[0] === "agents") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "agents", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "agents"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "sessions") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "sessions", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "sessions"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "audit") return ["app", "audit"];
    if (rest[0] === "roles") return ["app", "roles"];
    if (rest[0] === "enclaves") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "enclaves", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "enclaves"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "secrets") return ["app", "secrets"];
    if (rest[0] === "network") return ["app", "network"];
    if (rest[0] === "terminals") return ["app", "terminals"];
    if (rest[0] === "terminal") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "terminal", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "terminals"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "approvals") return ["app", "approvals"];
    if (rest[0] === "account") return ["app", "account"];
    if (rest[0] === "skills") return ["app", "skills"];
    if (rest[0] === "templates") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "templates", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "templates"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "workflows") {
        if (rest.length > 2) return ["app", "workflows", rest[1], rest[2]];
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["app", "workflows", rest[1]];
        return ["app", "workflows"];
    }
    return ["app"];
}

function normalizeConsumerParts(rest: string[]): string[] {
    if (rest.length === 0) return ["consumer"];
    if (rest[0] === "network") return ["consumer", "network"];
    if (rest[0] === "enclaves") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["consumer", "enclaves", rest[1]];
        return ["consumer", "enclaves"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "approvals") return ["consumer", "approvals"];
    if (rest[0] === "account") return ["consumer", "account"];
    return ["consumer"];
}

function normalizeOpsParts(rest: string[]): string[] {
    if (rest.length === 0) return ["ops", "admin", "testflight"];
    if (rest[0] === "fleet") {
        if (rest.length > 1) return ["ops", "fleet", rest[1]];
        return ["ops", "fleet"];
    }
    if (rest[0] === "network") return ["ops", "network"];
    if (rest[0] === "events") return ["ops", "events"];
    if (rest[0] === "improvement") return ["ops", "improvement"];
    if (rest[0] === "admin") return ["ops", "admin", normalizeOpsAdminSection(rest[1])];
```

```text
//go:build cdp

// CDP browser tests for the ncd web UI.
//
// These tests require a real Chrome/Chromium browser and a running ncd server.
// They are gated behind the "cdp" build tag to keep CI fast; run them with:
//
//	go test -v -tags cdp -run TestCDP ./cmd/ncd/ -cdp-url http://localhost:8090
//
// The server must be started with --dev --demo so that the dev-login bypass
// and in-memory demo state are available:
//
//	go run . serve --dev --demo --port 8090
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"image"
	_ "image/jpeg"
	_ "image/png"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/cookiejar"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"connectrpc.com/connect"
	"github.com/chromedp/chromedp"
	ncdadminv1 "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/admin/v1"
	ncdadminv1connect "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/admin/v1/ncdadminv1connect"
)

// cdpURL is the base URL of a running ncd server. Override with -cdp-url.
var cdpURL = flag.String("cdp-url", "http://localhost:8090", "ncd server base URL for CDP tests")

// cdpHeadless controls whether Chrome runs headless. Set -cdp-headless=false to watch.
var cdpHeadless = flag.Bool("cdp-headless", true, "run Chrome headless")

// newCDPCtx creates a browser context with a reasonable timeout and returns
// the context and a cancel function. Each test gets its own isolated browser
// context (tab) so tests are fully parallel-safe.
func newCDPCtx(t *testing.T, timeout time.Duration) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {
	t.Helper()

	opts := chromedp.DefaultExecAllocatorOptions[:]
	if exec := browserExecPath(); exec != "" {
		opts = append(opts, chromedp.ExecPath(exec))
	}
	if !*cdpHeadless {
		opts = append(opts, chromedp.Flag("headless", false))
	}

	allocCtx, allocCancel := chromedp.NewExecAllocator(context.Background(), opts...)
	t.Cleanup(allocCancel)

	browserCtx, browserCancel := chromedp.NewContext(allocCtx)

	ctx, timeoutCancel := context.WithTimeout(browserCtx, timeout)
	cancel := func() {
		timeoutCancel()
		browserCancel()
	}
	t.Cleanup(cancel)
	return ctx, cancel
}

// devLogin navigates to the dev login endpoint and waits for the app shell to
// appear. All tests that require authentication call this first.
func devLogin(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T) {
	t.Helper()
	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/auth/dev/login"),
		// After dev login the server redirects to the SPA.
```

```text
// TestCDP_APIKeyCreateAndRevoke exercises creating an API key through the UI
// and revoking it. Requires the account page to have a "Create API Key" flow.
func TestCDP_APIKeyCreateAndRevoke(t *testing.T) {
	ctx, _ := newCDPCtx(t, 45*time.Second)
	devLogin(ctx, t)

	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/app/account"),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(`button`, chromedp.ByQuery),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("account page: %v", err)
	}

	// Find and click "Create API Key" button.
	var clicked bool
	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Evaluate(`
			(function() {
				const btns = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button'));
				const btn = btns.find(b => b.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('api key') || b.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('create key'));
				if (btn) { btn.click(); return true; }
				return false;
			})()
		`, &clicked),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("find create api key button: %v", err)
	}
	if !clicked {
		t.Skip("Create API Key button not found on account page (feature may require live DB)")
	}

	// Fill in key name and submit.
	keyName := fmt.Sprintf("cdp-test-key-%d", time.Now().UnixNano()%10000)
	var submitted bool
	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Sleep(300*time.Millisecond),
		chromedp.Evaluate(fmt.Sprintf(`
			(function() {
				const inp = document.querySelector('input[type="text"], input[name="name"]');
				if (!inp) return false;
				inp.value = %q;
				inp.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true}));
				const btn = document.querySelector('button[type="submit"], dialog button[class*="primary"]');
				if (btn) { btn.click(); return true; }
				return false;
			})()
		`, keyName), &submitted),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("submit api key form: %v", err)
	}
	if !submitted {
		t.Log("API key form submit skipped — no submit button found")
	}
}

// TestCDP_SessionList verifies that the account page renders the current
// session in the sessions section.
func TestCDP_SessionList(t *testing.T) {
	ctx, _ := newCDPCtx(t, 30*time.Second)
	devLogin(ctx, t)

	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/app/account"),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(`body`, chromedp.ByQuery),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("account page load: %v", err)
	}

	body, err := getText(ctx, "body")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("body text: %v", err)
	}
	// The sessions section should mention "current" or list a session.
	if !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(body), "session") {
		t.Errorf("account page missing sessions section; body prefix: %.300s", body)
	}
}

// --------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Audit log workflow
// --------------------------------------------------------------------------

// TestCDP_AuditLog navigates to the audit log view and verifies the search
// interface renders without errors.
func TestCDP_AuditLog(t *testing.T) {
	ctx, _ := newCDPCtx(t, 30*time.Second)
	devLogin(ctx, t)

	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		navTo(ctx, "/app/audit", `input[type="text"], input[type="search"], [class*="search"], [class*="audit"]`),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("audit log page: %v", err)
	}
}

// the UI responds (either shows results or an empty state, not an error).
func TestCDP_AuditLogSearch(t *testing.T) {
```

```text
//go:build cdp

// Package main CDP tests using cdpscripttest.
//
// Each test scenario lives in testdata/cdp/*.txt as a txtar script.
// Scripts that need a running ncd server should call:
//
//	start-ncd-server
//
// at the top. This command ensures $BASE_URL points at a running ncd demo
// server. If the server is already reachable, it is a no-op. Otherwise it
// builds and starts one automatically.
//
// To point at an existing server:
//
//	go test -v -tags cdp -run TestCDPScript ./cmd/ncd/ -cdp-url http://localhost:8090
//
// or:
//
//	NCD_URL=http://localhost:8090 go test -v -tags cdp -run TestCDPScript ./cmd/ncd/
//
// Visual regression baselines live in testdata/cdp/baselines/ (committed).
// Regenerate with:
//
//	go test -update-golden -tags cdp -run TestCDPScript ./cmd/ncd/
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"sync"
//go:build cdp

// CDP browser tests for the ncd web UI.
//
// These tests require a real Chrome/Chromium browser and a running ncd server.
// They are gated behind the "cdp" build tag to keep CI fast; run them with:
//
//	go test -v -tags cdp -run TestCDP ./cmd/ncd/ -cdp-url http://localhost:8090
//
// The server must be started with --dev --demo so that the dev-login bypass
// and in-memory demo state are available:
//
//	go run . serve --dev --demo --port 8090
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"image"
	_ "image/jpeg"
	_ "image/png"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/cookiejar"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"connectrpc.com/connect"
	"github.com/chromedp/chromedp"
	ncdadminv1 "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/admin/v1"
	ncdadminv1connect "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/admin/v1/ncdadminv1connect"
)

// cdpURL is the base URL of a running ncd server. Override with -cdp-url.
```

```text
func browserExecPath() string {
	if p := os.Getenv("CDP_BROWSER"); p != "" {
		return p
	}
	candidates := []string{
		"/Applications/Brave Browser.app/Contents/MacOS/Brave Browser",
		"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome",
		"/Applications/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium",
	}
	for _, p := range candidates {
		if _, err := os.Stat(p); err == nil {
			return p
		}
	}
	return "" // fall back to chromedp default (google-chrome in PATH)
}

// ncdServerProcess holds the shared demo server state. Started at most once
// per test run via ensureNCDServer.
var ncdServerProcess struct {
	once    sync.Once
	url     string
	err     error
	cmd     *exec.Cmd
	binPath string
}

func nextFreePort() (int, error) {
	ln, err := net.Listen("tcp", "127.0.0.1:0")
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	defer ln.Close()
	return ln.Addr().(*net.TCPAddr).Port, nil
}

func managedNCDEnv() []string {
	// Keep CDP startup deterministic in environments that export OAuth creds.
	// In demo mode we don't need external provider initialization.
```

## Assistant (final_answer)

> I verified the live flow with both automation paths.
>
> CDP verification:
> - `go test -v -tags cdp -run 'TestCDP_(DevLogin|AccountPage)$' ./cmd/ncd -cdp-url http://127.0.0.1:8090`
> - Both `TestCDP_DevLogin` and `TestCDP_AccountPage` passed against the running local server.
>
> AXMCP verification:
> - Launched the local app in Brave at `http://127.0.0.1:8090/app/account`
> - Confirmed dev login succeeded and the authenticated Account screen rendered
> - Clicked `Upgrade Plan` and `Manage Billing` in the real UI
>
> Observed end-to-end behavior:
> - `Upgrade Plan` surfaces: “Checkout unavailable … stripe customer creation … You need to provide your API key…”
> - `Manage Billing` surfaces: “Billing portal unavailable … stripe customer creation failed … You did not provide an API key…”
>
> So the application flow itself is wired correctly through auth, Account, and the billing actions, but this environment is not configured for a real Stripe checkout or Apple Pay presentation. There are no `STRIPE_*` env vars in the current shell, so the flow stops at the Stripe boundary before redirecting to Checkout.
>
> To verify actual Apple Pay end to end, the remaining prerequisites are operational rather than code-level:
> - Stripe API keys and billing config
> - a valid Stripe price/customer path
> - Apple Pay enabled in Stripe for the deployed config
> - a verified domain
> - Safari/iPhone with Wallet configured
>
> No code changes were needed for this verification pass.

```text
package billing

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82"
	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/billingportal/session"
	checkoutsession "github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/checkout/session"
	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/customer"
)

// GetCustomer retrieves a Stripe customer by ID.
func (s *InternalService) GetCustomer(id string) (*stripe.Customer, error) {
	params := &stripe.CustomerParams{}
	params.AddExpand("subscriptions")
	c, err := customer.Get(id, params)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("stripe get customer: %w", err)
	}
	return c, nil
}

// ListPaymentMethods returns payment methods for a Stripe customer.
func (s *InternalService) ListPaymentMethods(customerID string) ([]*stripe.PaymentMethod, error) {
	params := &stripe.CustomerListPaymentMethodsParams{
		Customer: stripe.String(customerID),
	}
	iter := customer.ListPaymentMethods(params)
	var methods []*stripe.PaymentMethod
	for iter.Next() {
		methods = append(methods, iter.PaymentMethod())
	}
	if err := iter.Err(); err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("stripe list payment methods: %w", err)
	}
	return methods, nil
}

type InternalService struct {
	key string
}

func NewService(key string) *InternalService {
	stripe.Key = key
	return &InternalService{key: key}
}

func (s *InternalService) CreateCustomer(email, name string) (string, error) {
	params := &stripe.CustomerParams{
		Email: stripe.String(email),
		Name:  stripe.String(name),
	}
	c, err := customer.New(params)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("stripe customer creation failed: %w", err)
	}
	return c.ID, nil
}

func (s *InternalService) CreateCheckoutSession(customerID, successURL, cancelURL, priceID string) (string, error) {
	params := &stripe.CheckoutSessionParams{
		Customer:   stripe.String(customerID),
		SuccessURL: stripe.String(successURL),
		CancelURL:  stripe.String(cancelURL),
		// Omit PaymentMethodTypes to let Stripe auto-enable all configured
		// methods (card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Link).
		Mode: stripe.String(string(stripe.CheckoutSessionModeSubscription)),
		LineItems: []*stripe.CheckoutSessionLineItemParams{
			{
				Price:    stripe.String(priceID),
				Quantity: stripe.Int64(1),
			},
		},
	}

	sess, err := checkoutsession.New(params)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("checkout session creation failed: %w", err)
	}
	return sess.URL, nil
}

func (s *InternalService) CreatePortalSession(customerID, returnURL string) (string, error) {
	params := &stripe.BillingPortalSessionParams{
		Customer:  stripe.String(customerID),
		ReturnURL: stripe.String(returnURL),
	}
	sess, err := session.New(params)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("portal session creation failed: %w", err)
	}
	return sess.URL, nil
}
```

## Assistant

```text
func (s *nanoclawServer) RevokeAPIKey(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.RevokeAPIKeyRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.RevokeAPIKeyResponse], error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("database not initialized"))
	}
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}

	if err := s.store.DeleteAPIKey(ctx, req.Msg.KeyId, user.ID); err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeNotFound, err)
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.RevokeAPIKeyResponse{}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) CreateCheckoutSession(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.CreateCheckoutSessionRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.CreateCheckoutSessionResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}
	if s.billing == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("billing not configured"))
	}
	customerID, err := s.ensureStripeCustomer(ctx, user)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	priceID := strings.TrimSpace(req.Msg.PriceId)
	if priceID == "" {
		priceID = s.stripeDefaultPrice
	}
	if priceID == "" {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("price_id is required"))
	}

	url, err := s.billing.CreateCheckoutSession(customerID, req.Msg.SuccessUrl, req.Msg.CancelUrl, priceID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.CreateCheckoutSessionResponse{
		CheckoutUrl: url,
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) CreateBillingPortalSession(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.CreateBillingPortalSessionRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.CreateBillingPortalSessionResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}
	if s.billing == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("billing not configured"))
	}
	customerID, err := s.ensureStripeCustomer(ctx, user)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	url, err := s.billing.CreatePortalSession(customerID, req.Msg.ReturnUrl)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.CreateBillingPortalSessionResponse{
		PortalUrl: url,
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) ensureStripeCustomer(ctx context.Context, user *db.User) (string, error) {
	if user == nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("user is required")
	}
	if user.StripeCustomerID != "" {
		return user.StripeCustomerID, nil
	}
	if s.billing == nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("billing not configured")
	}
	if s.store == nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("database not initialized")
	}
	custID, err := s.billing.CreateCustomer(user.Email, user.Name)
```

## Assistant (commentary)

> The main gap is straightforward: there’s no non-Stripe billing backend for local verification. I’m wiring a dev/demo billing service that returns local hosted pages and fake payment methods so the browser path behaves like a real checkout loop.

```text
func (s *nanoclawServer) ListPaymentMethods(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}
	if s.billing == nil || user.StripeCustomerID == "" {
		return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsResponse{}), nil
	}

	methods, err := s.billing.ListPaymentMethods(user.StripeCustomerID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	var pms []*ncdv1.PaymentMethod
	for _, m := range methods {
		pm := &ncdv1.PaymentMethod{Id: m.ID}
		if m.Card != nil {
			pm.Last4 = m.Card.Last4
			pm.Brand = string(m.Card.Brand)
			pm.ExpMonth = int32(m.Card.ExpMonth)
			pm.ExpYear = int32(m.Card.ExpYear)
		}
		pms = append(pms, pm)
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsResponse{
		PaymentMethods: pms,
	}), nil
}
```

```text
func (s *nanoclawServer) GetAccountDetails(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdadminv1.GetAccountDetailsRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdadminv1.GetAccountDetailsResponse], error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("database not initialized"))
	}
	u, err := s.store.GetUser(ctx, req.Msg.UserId)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeNotFound, fmt.Errorf("user not found: %w", err))
	}

	resp := &ncdadminv1.GetAccountDetailsResponse{
		Summary: &ncdadminv1.AdminAccountSummary{
			UserId:    u.ID,
			Email:     u.Email,
			CreatedAt: timestamppb.New(u.CreatedAt),
			Suspended: u.Suspended,
		},
	}

	if s.billing != nil && u.StripeCustomerID != "" {
		if cust, err := s.billing.GetCustomer(u.StripeCustomerID); err == nil {
			resp.StripeCustomerId = cust.ID
			resp.StripeBalance = cust.Balance
			if cust.Subscriptions != nil {
				for _, sub := range cust.Subscriptions.Data {
					resp.Subscriptions = append(resp.Subscriptions, &ncdadminv1.SubscriptionSummary{
						Id:                sub.ID,
						Status:            string(sub.Status),
						CancelAtPeriodEnd: sub.CancelAtPeriodEnd,
					})
				}
			}
		} else {
			slog.Error("failed to fetch stripe customer", "customer_id", u.StripeCustomerID, "error", err)
		}
		if methods, err := s.billing.ListPaymentMethods(u.StripeCustomerID); err == nil {
			for _, pm := range methods {
				info := &ncdadminv1.PaymentMethodInfo{
					Id:   pm.ID,
					Type: string(pm.Type),
				}
				if pm.Card != nil {
					info.CardBrand = string(pm.Card.Brand)
					info.CardLast_4 = pm.Card.Last4
					info.CardExpMonth = int32(pm.Card.ExpMonth)
					info.CardExpYear = int32(pm.Card.ExpYear)
				}
				resp.PaymentMethods = append(resp.PaymentMethods, info)
			}
		} else {
			slog.Error("failed to fetch payment methods", "customer_id", u.StripeCustomerID, "error", err)
		}
	}
```

```text
import { h, Fragment } from "preact";
import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "preact/hooks";
import { ncdClient, accountClient } from "./client";
import { getAuthState, logout, formatFreeTier } from "./auth";
import { formatTimestamp } from "./util";
import type { Session, APIKeyInfo, PaymentMethod } from "./gen/ncd/v1/ncd_pb.js";
import type { DeletionStatus } from "./gen/ncd/account/v1/account_pb.js";
import { DeletionState } from "./gen/ncd/account/v1/account_pb.js";
import { notifyError, notifySuccess } from "./toast";
import { analytics, getSessionRecordingPreference, setSessionRecordingPreference } from "./analytics";
import { readHTTPErrorMessage } from "./http";

interface LinkedIdentity {
    provider: string;
    provider_sub: string;
    email: string;
    linked_at: string;
    last_sign_in_at: string;
}

interface PasskeyInfo {
    credential_id: string;
    friendly_name: string;
    created_at: string;
    last_used: string;
}

interface CheckoutSessionCreator {
    (request: {
        priceId: string;
        successUrl: string;
        cancelUrl: string;
    }): Promise<{ checkoutUrl: string }>;
}

interface BillingPortalSessionCreator {
    (request: {
        returnUrl: string;
    }): Promise<{ portalUrl: string }>;
}

interface CheckoutActionDeps {
    origin: string;
    createCheckoutSession: CheckoutSessionCreator;
    setBusy: (busy: boolean) => void;
    redirect: (url: string) => void;
    notifyError: (message: string) => void;
}

interface BillingPortalActionDeps {
    origin: string;
    createBillingPortalSession: BillingPortalSessionCreator;
    setBusy: (busy: boolean) => void;
    redirect: (url: string) => void;
    notifyError: (message: string) => void;
}

function accountBillingReturnUrl(origin: string): string {
    return origin + "/#/app/account";
}

export async function startCheckoutAction(deps: CheckoutActionDeps): Promise<void> {
    try {
        deps.setBusy(true);
        const res = await deps.createCheckoutSession({
            priceId: "",
            successUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
            cancelUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
        });
        deps.redirect(res.checkoutUrl);
    } catch (err: any) {
        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
        deps.notifyError("Checkout unavailable: " + message);
        deps.setBusy(false);
    }
}

export async function openBillingPortalAction(deps: BillingPortalActionDeps): Promise<void> {
    try {
        deps.setBusy(true);
        const res = await deps.createBillingPortalSession({
            returnUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
        });
        deps.redirect(res.portalUrl);
    } catch (err: any) {
        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
        deps.notifyError("Billing portal unavailable: " + message);
        deps.setBusy(false);
    }
}

export function AccountView() {
    const auth = getAuthState();
    const [sessions, setSessions] = useState<Session[]>([]);
    const [apiKeys, setApiKeys] = useState<APIKeyInfo[]>([]);
    const [paymentMethods, setPaymentMethods] = useState<PaymentMethod[]>([]);
    const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    const [loggingOut, setLoggingOut] = useState(false);

    const [portalBusy, setPortalBusy] = useState(false);

    const [identities, setIdentities] = useState<LinkedIdentity[]>([]);
    const [passkeys, setPasskeys] = useState<PasskeyInfo[]>([]);
    const [registeringPasskey, setRegisteringPasskey] = useState(false);
    const [deletionStatus, setDeletionStatus] = useState<DeletionStatus | null>(null);
```

```text
Chunk ID: cffba8

Output:
992:type nanoclawServer struct {
func (s *nanoclawServer) Status(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[nanoclawv1.StatusRequest]) (*connect.Response[nanoclawv1.StatusResponse], error) {
	return connect.NewResponse(&nanoclawv1.StatusResponse{
		Status:      "ok",
		UptimeSince: timestamppb.New(s.startTime),
	}), nil
}

// NanoclawControllerService implementation.

func (s *nanoclawServer) GetAccount(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.GetAccountRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.GetAccountResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, fmt.Errorf("not authenticated"))
	}

	remaining := int64(freeTierLimitSeconds) - user.FreeTierUsedSeconds
	if remaining < 0 {
		remaining = 0
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.GetAccountResponse{
		UserId:                   user.ID,
		Email:                    user.Email,
		Name:                     user.Name,
		PictureUrl:               user.PictureURL,
		Tier:                     tierForUser(user),
		FreeTierRemainingSeconds: remaining,
		HasPaymentMethod:         user.StripeCustomerID != "",
		CreatedAt:                timestamppb.New(user.CreatedAt),
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) UpdateAccount(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.UpdateAccountRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.UpdateAccountResponse], error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("database not initialized"))
	}
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, fmt.Errorf("not authenticated"))
	}
	name := strings.TrimSpace(req.Msg.Name)
	if name == "" {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("name is required"))
	}
	if utf8.RuneCountInString(name) > 120 {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("name is too long"))
	}
	if err := s.store.UpdateUserName(ctx, user.ID, name); err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, fmt.Errorf("update account: %w", err))
	}
	refreshed, err := s.store.GetUser(ctx, user.ID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, fmt.Errorf("get updated account: %w", err))
	}
	remaining := int64(freeTierLimitSeconds) - refreshed.FreeTierUsedSeconds
	if remaining < 0 {
		remaining = 0
	}
	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.UpdateAccountResponse{
		Account: &ncdv1.GetAccountResponse{
			UserId:                   refreshed.ID,
			Email:                    refreshed.Email,
			Name:                     refreshed.Name,
			PictureUrl:               refreshed.PictureURL,
			Tier:                     tierForUser(refreshed),
			FreeTierRemainingSeconds: remaining,
			HasPaymentMethod:         refreshed.StripeCustomerID != "",
			CreatedAt:                timestamppb.New(refreshed.CreatedAt),
		},
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) StartDeviceAuth(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.StartDeviceAuthRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.StartDeviceAuthResponse], error) {
	if s.deviceFlowConfigErr != "" {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("%s", s.deviceFlowConfigErr))
	}
```

html.EscapeString(sess.PriceID), html.EscapeString(sess.SuccessURL), html.EscapeString(sess.CancelURL))\n+\tcase http.MethodPost:\n+\t\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n+\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid form\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n+\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t}\n+\t\taction := strings.TrimSpace(r.Form.Get(\"action\"))\n+\t\tvar redirectURL string\n+\t\tvar err error\n+\t\tswitch action {\n+\t\tcase \"complete\":\n+\t\t\tredirectURL, err = demo.CompleteCheckout(sessionID)\n+\t\tcase \"cancel\":\n+\t\t\tredirectURL, err = demo.CancelCheckout(sessionID)\n+\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid action\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n+\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusNotFound)\n+\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t}\n+\t\thttp.Redirect(w, r, redirectURL, http.StatusSeeOther)\n+\tdefault:\n+\t\thttp.Error(w, \"method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+func (s *nanoclawServer) handleDemoBillingPortal(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n+\tdemo := s.demoBilling()\n+\tif demo == nil {\n+\t\thttp.NotFound(w, r)\n+\t\treturn\n+\t}\n+\tsessionID := strings.TrimSpace(r.URL.Query().Get(\"session_id\"))\n+\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n+\t\thttp.Error(w, \"missing session_id\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n+\t\treturn\n+\t}\n+\tswitch r.Method {\n+\tcase http.MethodGet:\n+\t\tsess, ok := demo.GetPortalSession(sessionID)\n+\t\tif !ok {\n+\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"portal session not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n+\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tmethods, err := demo.ListPaymentMethods(sess.CustomerID)\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusNotFound)\n+\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tmethodList := \"No payment methods on file.\"\n+\t\tif len(methods) \u003e 0 {\n+\t\t\tparts := make([]string, 0, len(methods))\n+\t\t\tfor _, pm := range methods {\n+\t\t\t\tif pm == nil || pm.Card == nil {\n+\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s ending in %s\", pm.Card.Brand, pm.Card.Last4))\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif len(parts) \u003e 0 {\n+\t\t\t\tmethodList = strings.Join(parts, \"\u003cbr\u003e\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n+\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, demoPortalHTML, html.EscapeString(sess.ID), methodList, html.EscapeString(sess.ReturnURL))\n+\tcase http.MethodPost:\n+\t\treturnURL, err := demo.PortalReturnURL(sessionID)\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusNotFound)\n+\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t}\n+\t\thttp.Redirect(w, r, returnURL, http.StatusSeeOther)\n+\tdefault:\n+\t\thttp.Error(w, \"method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+const demoCheckoutHTML = `\u003c!doctype html\u003e\n+\u003chtml lang=\"en\"\u003e\n+\u003chead\u003e\n+\u003cmeta charset=\"utf-8\"\u003e\n+\u003cmeta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1\"\u003e\n+\u003ctitle\u003eNanoclaw Demo Checkout\u003c/title\u003e\n+\u003cstyle\u003e\n+body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; margin: 0; background: #f6f7fb; color: #172033; }\n+main { max-width: 720px; margin: 64px auto; background: #fff; border-radius: 18px; box-shadow: 0 12px 40px rgba(23,32,51,.12); padding: 32px; }\n+.eyebrow { color: #5a6785; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .08em; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 12px; }\n+h1 { margin: 0 0 12px; font-size: 32px; }\n+p, li { line-height: 1.5; }\n+.card { border: 1px solid #dde2ee; border-radius: 14px; padding: 16px; margin: 20px 0; background: #fbfcfe; }\n+.actions { display: flex; gap: 12px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-top: 28px; }\n+button { border: 0; border-radius: 999px; padding: 14px 22px; font-size: 15px; cursor: pointer; }\n+.primary { background: #111827; color: #fff; }\n+.secondary { background: #e9edf5; color: #172033; }\n+\u003c/style\u003e\n+\u003c/head\u003e\n+\u003cbody\u003e\n+\u003cmain\u003e\n+\u003cdiv class=\"eyebrow\"\u003eDemo Hosted Checkout\u003c/div\u003e\n+\u003ch1\u003ePay with Apple Pay\u003c/h1\u003e\n+\u003cp\u003eThis local checkout page simulates the Stripe-hosted subscription flow so the full browser redirect loop can be exercised in dev mode.\u003c/p\u003e\n+\u003cdiv class=\"card\"\u003e\n+\u003cstrong\u003eSession:\u003c/strong\u003e %s\u003cbr\u003e\n+\u003cstrong\u003ePrice:\u003c/strong\u003e %s\n+\u003c/div\u003e\n+\u003cul\u003e\n+\u003cli\u003eSuccess URL: %s\u003c/li\u003e\n+\u003cli\u003eCancel URL: %s\u003c/li\u003e\n+\u003c/ul\u003e\n+\u003cform method=\"post\"\u003e\n+\u003cdiv class=\"actions\"\u003e\n+\u003cbutton class=\"primary\" type=\"submit\" name=\"action\" value=\"complete\"\u003eApple Pay (Demo)\u003c/button\u003e\n+\u003cbutton class=\"secondary\" type=\"submit\" name=\"action\"
```text
Command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,220p' cmd/ncd/billing/metering.go"
Chunk ID: e30483
Wall time: 0.0000 seconds
Process exited with code 0
Original token count: 803
Output:
package billing

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
)

// Meter aggregates usage records and reports them to Stripe.
type Meter struct {
	store    db.Store
	billing  *InternalService
	interval time.Duration

	mu               sync.Mutex
	checkpointLoaded bool
	lastReported     time.Time
}

const meterCheckpointName = "billing_meter_v1"

// NewMeter creates a usage metering reporter.
func NewMeter(store db.Store, billing *InternalService) *Meter {
	return &Meter{
		store:    store,
		billing:  billing,
		interval: 1 * time.Hour,
	}
}

// SetInterval overrides the default metering reporting interval.
func (m *Meter) SetInterval(d time.Duration) {
	if d <= 0 {
		return
	}
	m.interval = d
}

// Run starts the metering loop. It blocks until ctx is cancelled.
func (m *Meter) Run(ctx context.Context) {
	ticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)
	defer ticker.Stop()

	for {
		select {
		case <-ctx.Done():
			return
		case <-ticker.C:
			if err := m.reportUsage(ctx); err != nil {
				slog.Error("metering report failed", "error", err)
			}
		}
	}
}

func (m *Meter) reportUsage(ctx context.Context) error {
	since, err := m.reportingWindowStart(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	now := time.Now().UTC()
	var (
		pageToken string
		reported  int
	)
	for {
		users, nextToken, err := m.store.ListUsers(ctx, 100, pageToken)
		if err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("list users: %w", err)
		}
		for _, u := range users {
			// Metering only applies to customers with Stripe subscriptions.
			if u.StripeCustomerID == "" {
				continue
			}
			computeSeconds, storageBytes, err := m.store.UsageSummary(ctx, u.ID, since)
			if err != nil {
				return fmt.Errorf("usage summary %s: %w", u.ID, err)
			}
			if computeSeconds == 0 && storageBytes == 0 {
				continue
			}
			// Keep logs deterministic and structured so this can be shipped to a
			// metering bridge worker until Stripe meter event IDs are configured.
			slog.Info("metering: usage reported",
				"customer", u.StripeCustomerID, "user", u.ID, "since", since.Format(time.RFC3339),
				"compute_seconds", computeSeconds, "storage_bytes", storageBytes)
			reported++
		}
		if nextToken == "" {
			break
		}
		pageToken = nextToken
	}
	if err := m.store.SetProjectorCheckpoint(ctx, meterCheckpointName, now.Unix()); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("set meter checkpoint: %w", err)
	}
	m.mu.Lock()
	m.lastReported = now
	m.checkpointLoaded = true
	m.mu.Unlock()
	slog.Info("metering: report complete", "reported", reported, "since", since.Format(time.RFC3339))
	return nil
}

func (m *Meter) reportingWindowStart(ctx context.Context) (time.Time, error) {
	m.mu.Lock()
	if m.checkpointLoaded {
		since := m.lastReported
		m.mu.Unlock()
		return since, nil
	}
	m.mu.Unlock()

	lastOffset, err := m.store.GetProjectorCheckpoint(ctx, meterCheckpointName)
	if err != nil {
		return time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("get meter checkpoint: %w", err)
	}
	now := time.Now().UTC()
	since := now.Add(-m.interval)
	if lastOffset > 0 {
		t := time.Unix(lastOffset, 0).UTC()
		if t.Before(now) {
			since = t
		}
	}

	m.mu.Lock()
	m.lastReported = since
	m.checkpointLoaded = true
	m.mu.Unlock()
	return since, nil
}
```

```text
<title>Nanoclaw Demo Billing Portal</title>

main { max-width: 720px; margin: 64px auto; background: #fff; border-radius: 18px; box-shadow: 0 12px 40px rgba(23,32,51,.12); padding: 32px; }
.eyebrow { color: #5a6785; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .08em; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 12px; }
h1 { margin: 0 0 12px; font-size: 32px; }
.card { border: 1px solid #dde2ee; border-radius: 14px; padding: 16px; margin: 20px 0; background: #fbfcfe; }
button { border: 0; border-radius: 999px; padding: 14px 22px; font-size: 15px; cursor: pointer; background: #111827; color: #fff; margin-top: 28px; }
</style>
</head>

<strong>Session:</strong> %s
</div>

<button type="submit">Return to account</button>
</form>
</main>
</body>
</html>
`

func (s *nanoclawServer) Status(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[nanoclawv1.StatusRequest]) (*connect.Response[nanoclawv1.StatusResponse], error) {
	return connect.NewResponse(&nanoclawv1.StatusResponse{
		Status:      "ok",
		UptimeSince: timestamppb.New(s.startTime),
	}), nil
}

// NanoclawControllerService implementation.

func (s *nanoclawServer) GetAccount(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.GetAccountRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.GetAccountResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, fmt.Errorf("not authenticated"))
	}

	remaining := int64(freeTierLimitSeconds) - user.FreeTierUsedSeconds
	if remaining < 0 {
		remaining = 0
	}
```

```text
    async function handleCancelDeletion() {
        if (!deletionStatus?.cancellationToken) return;
        setDeletionBusy(true);
        try {
            const res = await accountClient.cancelAccountDeletion({
                cancellationToken: deletionStatus.cancellationToken,
            });
            setDeletionStatus(res.status ?? null);
            analytics.accountDeletionCancelled();

    function gracePeriodCountdown(purgeAt: { seconds?: bigint } | undefined): string {
        if (!purgeAt?.seconds) return "";
        const ms = Number(purgeAt.seconds) * 1000 - Date.now();
        if (ms <= 0) return "expired";
        const days = Math.floor(ms / 86400000);
        const hours = Math.floor((ms % 86400000) / 3600000);
        return days > 0 ? `${days}d ${hours}h remaining` : `${hours}h remaining`;
    }

    async function handleLogout() {
        setLoggingOut(true);
        await logout();
    }

    const b64urlToBytes = (input: string): Uint8Array => {
        const base64 = input.replace(/-/g, "+").replace(/_/g, "/");
        const padded = base64 + "=".repeat((4 - (base64.length % 4)) % 4);
        const bin = atob(padded);
        const out = new Uint8Array(bin.length);
        for (let i = 0; i < bin.length; i++) out[i] = bin.charCodeAt(i);
        return out;
    };

    const bytesToB64url = (bytes: ArrayBuffer | Uint8Array): string => {
        const arr = bytes instanceof Uint8Array ? bytes : new Uint8Array(bytes);
        let s = "";
        for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) s += String.fromCharCode(arr[i]);
        return btoa(s).replace(/\+/g, "-").replace(/\//g, "_").replace(/=+$/g, "");
    };

    async function registerPasskey() {
        if (!window.PublicKeyCredential || !navigator.credentials) {
            notifyError("Passkeys are not supported in this browser.");
            return;
        }
        setRegisteringPasskey(true);

            if (!optionsResp.ok) throw new Error(await readHTTPErrorMessage(optionsResp, "failed to initialize passkey registration"));
```

## Assistant (commentary)

> The manual AX click path is noisy to debug against the live SPA, so I’m codifying the whole checkout/portal loop as a CDP browser test. That gives us a deterministic end-to-end proof of the exact flow we just implemented, and then I’ll recheck the visible browser state.

err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"demo checkout page: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !clicked {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"demo checkout page did not render Apple Pay button\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.Evaluate(`\n+\t\t\t(function() {\n+\t\t\t\tconst btn = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(\"button\")).find(b =\u003e b.textContent.includes(\"Apple Pay (Demo)\"));\n+\t\t\t\tif (!btn) return false;\n+\t\t\t\tbtn.click();\n+\t\t\t\treturn true;\n+\t\t\t})()\n+\t\t`, \u0026clicked),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"click demo apple pay: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !clicked {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"Apple Pay (Demo) button not found\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.WaitVisible(`body`, chromedp.ByQuery),\n+\t\tchromedp.Evaluate(`\n+\t\t\t(function() {\n+\t\t\t\treturn window.location.pathname === \"/app/account\";\n+\t\t\t})()\n+\t\t`, \u0026clicked),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"return to account: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !clicked {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"did not return to /app/account after demo checkout\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tbody, err := getText(ctx, \"body\")\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"body text after checkout: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(body, \"4242\") {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"account page missing demo payment method after checkout; body prefix: %.300s\", body)\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.Evaluate(`\n+\t\t\t(function() {\n+\t\t\t\tconst btn = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(\"button\")).find(b =\u003e b.textContent.includes(\"Manage Billing\"));\n+\t\t\t\tif (!btn) return false;\n+\t\t\t\tbtn.click();\n+\t\t\t\treturn true;\n+\t\t\t})()\n+\t\t`, \u0026clicked),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"click manage billing: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !clicked {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"Manage Billing button not found\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.WaitVisible(`body`, chromedp.ByQuery),\n+\t\tchromedp.Evaluate(`\n+\t\t\t(function() {\n+\t\t\t\tconst body = document.body ? document.body.innerText : \"\";\n+\t\t\t\treturn body.includes(\"Demo Billing Portal\") \u0026\u0026 body.includes(\"4242\");\n+\t\t\t})()\n+\t\t`, \u0026clicked),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"demo billing portal: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !clicked {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"demo billing portal did not render saved payment method\")\n+\t}\n+}\n \n // --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n // Audit log workflow\n // --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n*** End Patch\n"
```json
}
```

## Assistant (commentary)

> I found the actual blocker: the Account UI intentionally sends an empty `priceId`, and in dev mode there was no default price configured, so checkout was failing before redirect. I’m setting a safe local default for the demo billing backend and rerunning the flow.

```json
  "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go\n@@\n \tif stripeKey != \"\" {\n \t\tliveBiller = billing.NewService(stripeKey)\n \t\tbiller = liveBiller\n \t\tslog.Info(\"billing enabled: stripe configured\", \"default_price_set\", stripePriceID != \"\")\n \t} else if serveDev || serveDemoState {\n+\t\tif stripePriceID == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tstripePriceID = \"price_demo_local\"\n+\t\t}\n \t\tdemoBiller = billing.NewDemoService()\n \t\tbiller = demoBiller\n \t\tslog.Info(\"billing enabled: demo billing backend\", \"default_price_set\", stripePriceID != \"\")\n \t}\n*** End Patch\n"
}
```

## Assistant (commentary)

> I suspect the form-post round-trip is the unstable part of the local hosted flow. I’m simplifying the demo pages to use plain same-origin GET redirects for complete/cancel/return so the loop behaves more like a normal hosted checkout handoff.

```text
			ctx2, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
			defer cancel()
			if err := s.posthog.DeleteFlag(ctx2, id); err != nil {
				slog.Error("posthog write-through: delete flag failed", "posthog_id", id, "error", err)
			}
		}(phID)
	}
	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdadminv1.DeleteFeatureGateResponse{}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) EvaluateFeatureGate(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdadminv1.EvaluateFeatureGateRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdadminv1.EvaluateFeatureGateResponse], error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("database not initialized"))
	}
	if req.Msg.Key == "" {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("key is required"))
	}

	gate, err := s.store.GetFeatureGate(ctx, req.Msg.Key)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeNotFound, err)
	}

	// Kill switch overrides everything.
	if gate.KillSwitch {
		return connect.NewResponse(&ncdadminv1.EvaluateFeatureGateResponse{
			Enabled:       false,
			EffectiveMode: ncdadminv1.FeatureGateMode_FEATURE_GATE_MODE_OFF,
			Source:        "kill_switch",
		}), nil
	}

	// Check scope overrides in precedence order.
	overrides, _ := s.store.ListFeatureGateOverrides(ctx, req.Msg.Key)
	effectiveMode := ncdadminv1.FeatureGateMode(gate.Mode)
	source := "global"

	// Scope precedence: user > group > workspace > tenant > global.
	scopePriority := map[string]int{"user": 5, "group": 4, "workspace": 3, "tenant": 2, "global": 1}
	bestPriority := 1

	for _, o := range overrides {
		if o.ScopeID == req.Msg.ScopeId || o.ScopeID == req.Msg.SubjectId {
			p := scopePriority[o.ScopeKind]
			if p > bestPriority {
				bestPriority = p
				effectiveMode = ncdadminv1.FeatureGateMode(o.Mode)
				source = o.ScopeKind + "/" + o.ScopeID
			}
		}
	}

	enabled := effectiveMode == ncdadminv1.FeatureGateMode_FEATURE_GATE_MODE_ON
	if enabled && gate.RolloutPercent < 100 && gate.RolloutPercent > 0 {
		// Deterministic cohort hash.
		h := fnv32a(req.Msg.Key + ":" + req.Msg.SubjectId)
		cohort := int(h % 100)
		if cohort >= gate.RolloutPercent {
			enabled = false
			source += " (rollout excluded)"
		}
	}

	s.gateEvalMu.Lock()
	s.gateEvalSeq++
	s.gateEvals = append(s.gateEvals, &ncdadminv1.GateEvaluation{
		Id:            fmt.Sprintf("eval-%06d", s.gateEvalSeq),
```

```text
package auth

import (
	"context"
	"net/http"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"connectrpc.com/connect"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
)

type contextKey string

const userContextKey contextKey = "authenticated_user"

// UserFromContext returns the authenticated user from the request context.
func UserFromContext(ctx context.Context) (*db.User, bool) {
	u, ok := ctx.Value(userContextKey).(*db.User)
	return u, ok
}

// ContextWithUser returns a new context with the given user set as the
// authenticated user. Useful for plain HTTP handlers that authenticate
// outside of the Connect-RPC interceptor chain.
func ContextWithUser(ctx context.Context, user *db.User) context.Context {
	return context.WithValue(ctx, userContextKey, user)
}

// SessionIDFromContext returns the session ID from the request context.
func SessionIDFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
	v, _ := ctx.Value(contextKey("session_id")).(string)
	return v
}

// TokenHashFromContext returns the token hash from the request context.
func TokenHashFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
	v, _ := ctx.Value(contextKey("token_hash")).(string)
	return v
}

// publicProcedures lists RPC procedures that don't require authentication.
var publicProcedures = map[string]bool{
	"/ncd.v1.NanoclawControllerService/StartDeviceAuth":           true,
	"/ncd.v1.NanoclawControllerService/PollDeviceAuth":            true,
	"/ncd.testflight.v1.TestFlightService/RegisterForEarlyAccess": true,
	"/ncd.testflight.v1.TestFlightService/GetRegistrationStatus":  true,
}

const devSessionToken = "ncd_dev_token"

type authInterceptorConfig struct {
	allowDevSession bool
	rateLimiter     *RateLimiter
}

type AuthInterceptorOption func(*authInterceptorConfig)

// WithDevSessionFallback allows the special dev session token to authenticate
// as a synthetic development user.
func WithDevSessionFallback() AuthInterceptorOption {
	return func(cfg *authInterceptorConfig) {
		cfg.allowDevSession = true
	}
}

// WithRateLimiter enables per-IP rate limiting on authentication attempts.
func WithRateLimiter(rl *RateLimiter) AuthInterceptorOption {
	return func(cfg *authInterceptorConfig) {
		cfg.rateLimiter = rl
	}
}

// NewAuthInterceptor creates a Connect-RPC interceptor that validates session
// tokens and API keys from the Authorization header or ncd_session cookie.
// It applies to unary and server-streaming/bidi handler RPCs.
func NewAuthInterceptor(signer *SessionSigner, store db.Store, opts ...AuthInterceptorOption) connect.Interceptor {
	cfg := &authInterceptorConfig{}
	for _, opt := range opts {
		opt(cfg)
	}
	return authInterceptor{signer: signer, store: store, cfg: cfg}
}

type authInterceptor struct {
	signer *SessionSigner
	store  db.Store
	cfg    *authInterceptorConfig
}

func (a authInterceptor) WrapUnary(next connect.UnaryFunc) connect.UnaryFunc {
	return func(ctx context.Context, req connect.AnyRequest) (connect.AnyResponse, error) {
		authCtx, err := a.authenticateRequest(ctx, req.Spec().Procedure, req.Header(), peerFromHeader(req.Header()))
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		return next(authCtx, req)
	}
}

func (a authInterceptor) WrapStreamingClient(next connect.StreamingClientFunc) connect.StreamingClientFunc {
	return next
}

func (a authInterceptor) WrapStreamingHandler(next connect.StreamingHandlerFunc) connect.StreamingHandlerFunc {
	return func(ctx context.Context, conn connect.StreamingHandlerConn) error {
		authCtx, err := a.authenticateRequest(ctx, conn.Spec().Procedure, conn.RequestHeader(), peerFromHeader(conn.RequestHeader()))
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		return next(authCtx, conn)
	}
}

func (a authInterceptor) authenticateRequest(ctx context.Context, procedure string, header http.Header, peerAddr string) (context.Context, error) {
	// Skip auth for public procedures.
```

## Assistant (commentary)

> I found the real bug. In dev mode, the `ncd_dev_token` bypass always injects a synthetic in-memory user, so the Stripe customer ID you create during checkout is lost on the next request. I’m fixing that path so the dev session resolves through the store when available, then the post-checkout Account refresh should pick up the saved payment method correctly.
