// AuthenticateToken validates a raw session token or API key and returns a
// context with the authenticated user set. Useful for plain HTTP handlers
// that cannot use the Connect-RPC interceptor.
func AuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string, signer *SessionSigner, store db.Store) (context.Context, error) {
	return authenticate(ctx, token, signer, store)
}

func authenticate(ctx context.Context, token string, signer *SessionSigner, store db.Store) (context.Context, error) {
	switch {
	case IsSessionToken(token):
		return authenticateSession(ctx, token, signer, store)
	case IsAPIKey(token):
		return authenticateAPIKey(ctx, token, store)
	default:
		return ctx, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}
}

func authenticateSession(ctx context.Context, token string, signer *SessionSigner, store db.Store) (context.Context, error) {
	sessionID, _, err := signer.ValidateToken(token)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx, err
	}

	// Check session exists in DB (enables revocation).
	tHash := TokenHash(token)
	sess, err := store.GetSessionByTokenHash(ctx, tHash)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx, err
	}

	// Touch session last_used.
	_ = store.TouchSession(ctx, sess.ID)

	user, err := store.GetUser(ctx, sess.UserID)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx, err
	}

	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, userContextKey, user)
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, contextKey("session_id"), sessionID)
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, contextKey("token_hash"), tHash)
	return ctx, nil
}

func authenticateAPIKey(ctx context.Context, token string, store db.Store) (context.Context, error) {
	prefix := APIKeyPrefix(token)
	if prefix == "" {
		return ctx, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}

	candidates, err := store.GetAPIKeysByPrefix(ctx, prefix)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx, err
	}

	for _, k := range candidates {
		if VerifyAPIKey(token, k.KeyHash) {
			_ = store.TouchAPIKey(ctx, k.ID)
			user, err := store.GetUser(ctx, k.UserID)
			if err != nil {
				return ctx, err
			}
			ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, userContextKey, user)
			return ctx, nil
		}
	}

	return ctx, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
}

// DevInterceptor returns an interceptor that injects a synthetic dev user
// into the request context, bypassing real authentication. Only use in dev mode.
func DevInterceptor() connect.Interceptor {
	return devInterceptor{user: devUser()}
}

type devInterceptor struct {
	user *db.User
}

func (d devInterceptor) WrapUnary(next connect.UnaryFunc) connect.UnaryFunc {
	return func(ctx context.Context, req connect.AnyRequest) (connect.AnyResponse, error) {
		return next(context.WithValue(ctx, userContextKey, d.user), req)
	}
}

func (d devInterceptor) WrapStreamingClient(next connect.StreamingClientFunc) connect.StreamingClientFunc {
```

// handleDevLogin creates a dev-mode session cookie without OAuth.
// Only registered when --dev is set.
func (s *nanoclawServer) handleDevLogin(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	if !s.dev {
		http.Redirect(w, r, "/?reauth=0", http.StatusTemporaryRedirect)
		return
	}
	http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "ncd_session",
		Value:    "ncd_dev_token",
		Path:     "/",
		HttpOnly: true,
		SameSite: http.SameSiteLaxMode,
		MaxAge:   30 * 24 * 60 * 60,
	})
	http.Redirect(w, r, "/#/app", http.StatusTemporaryRedirect)
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) handleDevLogout(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "ncd_session",
		Value:    "",
		Path:     "/",
		HttpOnly: true,
		SameSite: http.SameSiteLaxMode,
		MaxAge:   -1,
		Expires:  time.Unix(0, 0),
	})
	http.Redirect(w, r, "/", http.StatusTemporaryRedirect)
}

// handleAuthStatus returns JSON describing which auth methods are available.
func (s *nanoclawServer) handleAuthStatus(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	resp := struct {
		Google        bool   `json:"google"`
		Apple         bool   `json:"apple"`
		AppleClientID string `json:"apple_client_id,omitempty"`
		Passkey       bool   `json:"passkey"`

func (d devInterceptor) WrapStreamingClient(next connect.StreamingClientFunc) connect.StreamingClientFunc {

}

func (d devInterceptor) WrapStreamingHandler(next connect.StreamingHandlerFunc) connect.StreamingHandlerFunc {

func devUser() *db.User {
	return &db.User{
		ID:        "dev-user",
		Email:     "dev@localhost",
		Name:      "Dev User",
		CreatedAt: time.Now(),
	}
}

// peerFromRequest extracts the client IP from the request headers.
// Tries X-Forwarded-For, X-Real-Ip, then falls back to Peer-Addr.
func peerFromHeader(header http.Header) string {
	if xff := header.Get("X-Forwarded-For"); xff != "" {
		// First IP in the chain is the client.
		if comma := strings.Index(xff, ","); comma > 0 {
			return strings.TrimSpace(xff[:comma])
		}
		return strings.TrimSpace(xff)
	}
	if xri := header.Get("X-Real-Ip"); xri != "" {
		return strings.TrimSpace(xri)
	}
	// ConnectRPC doesn't expose peer address directly;
	// fall back to a header that reverse proxies typically set.
	return header.Get("Peer-Addr")
}
```

	otelInterceptor, err := otelconnect.NewInterceptor(
		otelconnect.WithTrustRemote(),             // propagate W3C trace context from clients
		otelconnect.WithoutServerPeerAttributes(), // Cloud Run rewrites IPs; drop noisy attrs

> I’m fixing the dev-auth path itself now so `ncd_dev_token` resolves the stored `dev-user` record instead of always fabricating a fresh one. That should preserve the Stripe customer ID and make the post-checkout Account page consistent.

## Assistant

### Tool: `apply_patch`

```json
{
  "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/auth/interceptor.go\n@@\n \tif token == \"\" {\n \t\tif a.cfg.rateLimiter != nil {\n \t\t\ta.cfg.rateLimiter.RecordFailure(peerAddr)\n \t\t}\n \t\treturn ctx, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)\n \t}\n \tif a.cfg.allowDevSession \u0026\u0026 token == devSessionToken {\n-\t\treturn context.WithValue(ctx, userContextKey, devUser()), nil\n+\t\treturn context.WithValue(ctx, userContextKey, devUserFromStore(ctx, a.store)), nil\n \t}\n@@\n func DevInterceptor() connect.Interceptor {\n-\treturn devInterceptor{user: devUser()}\n+\treturn devInterceptor{user: devUser()}\n }\n@@\n func devUser() *db.User {\n \treturn \u0026db.User{\n \t\tID:        \"dev-user\",\n \t\tEmail:     \"dev@localhost\",\n \t\tName:      \"Dev User\",\n \t\tCreatedAt: time.Now(),\n \t}\n }\n+\n+func devUserFromStore(ctx context.Context, store db.Store) *db.User {\n+\tif store == nil {\n+\t\treturn devUser()\n+\t}\n+\tu, err := store.GetUser(ctx, \"dev-user\")\n+\tif err == nil \u0026\u0026 u != nil {\n+\t\treturn u\n+\t}\n+\treturn devUser()\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"
}
```

## Assistant (commentary)

> The dev-user persistence bug was a little subtler: the store doesn’t guarantee the user ID is literally `dev-user`; it keys the synthetic login through an external identity. I’m switching the dev fallback lookup to resolve by identity instead of raw user ID.

## Assistant

### Tool: `apply_patch`

```json
{
  "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/auth/interceptor.go\n@@\n func devUserFromStore(ctx context.Context, store db.Store) *db.User {\n \tif store == nil {\n \t\treturn devUser()\n \t}\n-\tu, err := store.GetUser(ctx, \"dev-user\")\n+\tu, err := store.GetUserByIdentity(ctx, \"dev\", \"dev-user\")\n \tif err == nil \u0026\u0026 u != nil {\n \t\treturn u\n \t}\n \treturn devUser()\n }\n*** End Patch\n"
}
```

## Assistant (final_answer)

> I put the local end-to-end billing loop together.
>
> The main changes are in [serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go), [demo.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/demo.go), [interceptor.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/auth/interceptor.go), and [AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx). In `--dev` without Stripe credentials, `ncd` now uses a local hosted billing backend with:
> - demo checkout at `/dev/billing/checkout`
> - demo billing portal at `/dev/billing/portal`
> - a default local price so `Upgrade Plan` can succeed
> - a preserved dev identity in the store so the post-checkout Account page keeps the saved payment method state
>
> I also added coverage in [demo_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/demo_test.go) and extended the browser automation in [cdp_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/cdp_test.go) to exercise:
> - dev login
> - Account -> `Upgrade Plan`
> - hosted demo checkout -> `Apple Pay (Demo)`
> - return to Account with `4242` visible
> - `Manage Billing`
> - hosted demo portal with the saved method visible
>
> Verification I ran:
> - `go test ./cmd/ncd/auth ./cmd/ncd/billing ./cmd/ncd -run 'TestDemoServiceCheckoutAndPortal|TestCreate(CheckoutSession|BillingPortalSession)|TestDevInterceptor'`
> - `node --test cmd/ncd/ui/test/account-billing-actions.test.mjs`
> - `npm --prefix cmd/ncd/ui run build`
> - `go test -v -tags cdp -run 'TestCDP_AccountBillingDemoFlow$' ./cmd/ncd -cdp-url http://127.0.0.1:8090`
>
> That gives you a real local end-to-end billing flow now, but it is still a demo-hosted Apple Pay simulation, not real Stripe Apple Pay. The next step for production is still operational: wire real `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, `STRIPE_PRICE_ID`, domain verification, and then run the same browser path against real Checkout.

func TestDevInterceptor(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	interceptor := DevInterceptor()
	var capturedCtx context.Context

	next := interceptor.WrapUnary(func(ctx context.Context, req connect.AnyRequest) (connect.AnyResponse, error) {
		capturedCtx = ctx
		return connect.NewResponse(&ncdauditv1.GetAuditEntryRequest{}), nil
	})

	_, err := next(context.Background(), connect.NewRequest(&ncdauditv1.GetAuditEntryRequest{}))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("dev interceptor: %v", err)
	}

	user, ok := UserFromContext(capturedCtx)
	if !ok {
		t.Fatalf("no user in context")
	}
	if user.ID != "dev-user" {
		t.Fatalf("user id = %q, want %q", user.ID, "dev-user")
	}
	if user.Email != "dev@localhost" {
		t.Fatalf("user email = %q, want %q", user.Email, "dev@localhost")
	}
}
```

## Assistant (final_answer)

> I verified the Stripe and Safari side end to end.
>
> What’s true now:
> - Real Stripe test Checkout is wired from local `ncd`
> - Real Stripe Billing Portal is wired from local `ncd`
> - Stripe has an active Apple Pay payment-method domain for hosted Checkout:
>   - `checkout.stripe.com`
>   - `apple_pay.status = active`
> - In Safari, the live hosted Checkout page does show Apple Pay
> - The same Checkout in Brave did not show Apple Pay
>
> So the blocker was not the Nanoclaw checkout code, and not Stripe’s hosted-domain Apple Pay setup. The difference was client/browser eligibility.
>
> What I also confirmed:
> - The Stripe account is still not live-ready:
>   - `charges_enabled = false`
>   - `details_submitted = false`
> - That does not block test-mode Checkout rendering, but it will block live payments until the account is completed.
>
> I also created the Stripe test catalog needed for real subscription Checkout:
> - Product: `prod_UJUqFAFJvF3YC5`
> - Monthly recurring price: `price_1TKrqw9ZGEhtTeKUKd1YY4Jo`
>
> Code changes I made to support real verification:
> - [interceptor.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/auth/interceptor.go)
> - [serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go)
> - [auth_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/auth/auth_test.go)
>
> Practical conclusion:
> - Hosted Stripe Checkout + Apple Pay works for this app in Safari test mode right now.
> - The remaining production blockers are operational:
>   - finish Stripe account verification
>   - enable live charges
>   - use live keys and a live recurring price
>   - test again in Safari on an Apple Pay-capable device/browser
>
> If you want, I can do the next pass:
> 1. wire these Stripe values into a local `.env` template without committing secrets
> 2. add a focused CDP/automation note for “Safari is the Apple Pay validation browser”
> 3. clean up the temporary demo-billing fallback now that real Stripe test mode is working

## User

> do all of that, let's get this working and tested end to end

## Assistant (commentary)

> I’m turning the live Stripe/Safari findings into the repo’s working setup: a safe local env template, a concrete end-to-end runbook, and a cleanup pass on the dev billing path so test Stripe is the primary verification route while the demo backend remains a fallback.

# Nanoclaw Documentation Index

Status legend: `current` = reflects shipping code | `planned` = on roadmap with concrete plan | `aspirational` = product vision, not yet on roadmap | `legacy` = archived | `needs-update` = known stale

## How to Read This Tree

**New to Nanoclaw?** Start with [Architecture](architecture.md) for the full system overview, then [Roadmap](planning/roadmap.md) for where it's going.

**Building product?** Read the [OOUX index](planning/ooux/ooux-index.md) — objects, flows, screens, authorization.

**Building infrastructure?** Read the [OOIX index](planning/infrastructure/ooix/ooix-index.md) — topology, trust, operations.

**Reviewing security?** Start with [Security Audit](reviews/security_audit_2026_02_16.md), then the individual reviews.

---

## Architecture

|----------|--------|-------------|
| [architecture.md](architecture.md) | `current` | Core technical architecture: concepts, components, message flow, container execution, egress proxy, IPC/MCP, k8s deployment, security profiles |
| [product-description-architecture-audit.md](design/product-description-architecture-audit.md) | `current` | Consolidated product + architecture baseline from OOUX/OOIX/proto audit (coverage, gaps, and service wiring status) |

|----------|--------|-------------|

| [platform-adapter-guide.md](design/platform-adapter-guide.md) | `planned` | How to add a new chat platform adapter to Nanoclaw |

2026-04-10\n+---\n+\n+# Stripe Apple Pay End-to-End\n+\n+This document describes the current end-to-end Stripe billing path for Nanoclaw and the exact conditions required for Apple Pay to appear in hosted Stripe Checkout.\n+\n+## Current State\n+\n+Nanoclaw now supports two local billing verification modes:\n+\n+- Stripe-hosted Checkout and Billing Portal when `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` and `STRIPE_PRICE_ID` are set.\n+- Demo-hosted billing pages in `--dev` when Stripe is not configured.\n+\n+For real Apple Pay verification, use the Stripe-hosted path.\n+\n+## Local Setup\n+\n+Create a local `.env` from [`.env.example`](../../.env.example):\n+\n+```env\n+PORT=8090\n+STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...\n+STRIPE_PRICE_ID=price_...\n+STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...\n+```\n+\n+Start the server:\n+\n+```bash\n+go run ./cmd/ncd serve --dev\n+```\n+\n+Dev login is available at:\n+\n+```text\n+http://127.0.0.1:8090/auth/dev/login\n+```\n+\n+The Account page is:\n+\n+```text\n+http://127.0.0.1:8090/app/account\n+```\n+\n+## Stripe Test Catalog\n+\n+The current local verification setup was exercised with:\n+\n+- Product: `prod_UJUqFAFJvF3YC5`\n+- Monthly recurring price: `price_1TKrqw9ZGEhtTeKUKd1YY4Jo`\n+\n+Any active recurring price works for Checkout subscription mode.\n+\n+## Verified Behavior\n+\n+When Stripe is configured, Nanoclaw can:\n+\n+- create a hosted Checkout session from `Upgrade Plan`\n+- create a hosted Billing Portal session from `Manage Billing`\n+- redirect the browser to real Stripe-hosted pages\n+\n+Safari validation showed that Apple Pay appears in hosted Checkout for this account and test session.\n+\n+Brave validation showed Checkout, but Apple Pay did not appear there.\n+\n+## Apple Pay Requirements\n+\n+Apple Pay visibility depends on Stripe and client eligibility, not only Nanoclaw code.\n+\n+Required conditions:\n+\n+- Stripe Checkout must be used on a supported Apple browser/device, typically Safari.\n+- The customer device must have Apple Pay set up in Wallet.\n+- The relevant Stripe payment-method domain must be active.\n+- The Stripe account and payment configuration must allow the wallet flow.\n+\n+For hosted Checkout, the Stripe-side domain requirement is satisfied by Stripe's own domain:\n+\n+- `checkout.stripe.com`\n+\n+This account currently reports an active payment method domain for hosted Apple Pay on Stripe's domain.\n+\n+## Current Stripe Account Constraints\n+\n+The connected Stripe test account currently reports:\n+\n+- `charges_enabled = false`\n+- `details_submitted = false`\n+\n+This does not block test-mode Checkout rendering, but it does block production readiness.\n+\n+Before go-live:\n+\n+- complete Stripe business verification\n+- enable live charges\n+- create or select a live recurring price\n+- repeat Safari validation with live-mode credentials\n+\n+## Recommended Validation Matrix\n+\n+Run these checks whenever billing changes:\n+\n+1. Local server returns a real Checkout URL.\n+2. Local server returns a real Billing Portal URL.\n+3. Safari loads hosted Checkout.\n+4. Safari shows Apple Pay on the Checkout page.\n+5. Safari loads hosted Billing Portal.\n+6. Brave or Chrome loads Checkout without UI regressions, even if Apple Pay is absent.\n+\n+## Automation\n+\n+For deterministic browser verification, use the CDP test in [cdp_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/cdp_test.go):\n+\n+- `TestCDP_AccountBillingHostedRedirects`\n+\n+This test is opt-in and only runs when:\n+\n+- `NCD_CDP_LIVE_STRIPE=1`\n+- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` is set\n+- `STRIPE_PRICE_ID` is set\n+\n+Example:\n+\n+```bash\n+NCD_CDP_LIVE_STRIPE=1 \\\n+STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_... \\\n+STRIPE_PRICE_ID=price_... \\\n+go test -v -tags cdp -run 'TestCDP_AccountBillingHostedRedirects$' ./cmd/ncd -cdp-url http://127.0.0.1:8090\n+```\n*** End Patch\n"
}
````

err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"account page load: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\n+\tvar clicked bool\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.Evaluate(`\n+\t\t\t(function() {\n+\t\t\t\tconst btn = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(\"button\")).find(b =\u003e b.textContent.includes(\"Upgrade Plan\"));\n+\t\t\t\tif (!btn) return false;\n+\t\t\t\tbtn.click();\n+\t\t\t\treturn true;\n+\t\t\t})()\n+\t\t`, \u0026clicked),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"click upgrade plan: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !clicked {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"Upgrade Plan button not found\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tvar loc string\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.Sleep(2500*time.Millisecond),\n+\t\tchromedp.Location(\u0026loc),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"read checkout redirect location: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(loc, \"checkout.stripe.com\") {\n+\t\tbody, _ := getText(ctx, \"body\")\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected Stripe Checkout redirect, got %s body prefix: %.300s\", loc, body)\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+\"/app/account\"),\n+\t\tchromedp.WaitVisible(`button`, chromedp.ByQuery),\n+\t\tchromedp.Evaluate(`\n+\t\t\t(function() {\n+\t\t\t\tconst btn = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(\"button\")).find(b =\u003e b.textContent.includes(\"Manage Billing\"));\n+\t\t\t\tif (!btn) return false;\n+\t\t\t\tbtn.click();\n+\t\t\t\treturn true;\n+\t\t\t})()\n+\t\t`, \u0026clicked),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"click manage billing: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !clicked {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"Manage Billing button not found\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif err := chromedp.Run(ctx,\n+\t\tchromedp.Sleep(2500*time.Millisecond),\n+\t\tchromedp.Location(\u0026loc),\n+\t); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"read billing portal redirect location: %v\", err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(loc, \"billing.stripe.com\") {\n+\t\tbody, _ := getText(ctx, \"body\")\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected Stripe Billing Portal redirect, got %s body prefix: %.300s\", loc, body)\n+\t}\n+}\n \n // --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n // Audit log workflow\n // --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n*** End Patch\n"

package billing

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82"
	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/webhook"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/internal/httpx"
)

// WebhookHandler processes Stripe webhook events.
type WebhookHandler struct {
	secret string
	store  db.Store
}

// NewWebhookHandler creates a webhook handler with the given signing secret.
func NewWebhookHandler(secret string, store db.Store) *WebhookHandler {
	return &WebhookHandler{secret: secret, store: store}
}

// ServeHTTP handles incoming Stripe webhook requests.
func (h *WebhookHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
		httpx.WriteMethodNotAllowed(w, r, http.MethodPost)
		return
	}

	body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r.Body, 1<<16))
	if err != nil {
		httpx.WriteBadRequest(w, r, "webhook_read_body_failed", "invalid request body", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	event, err := webhook.ConstructEvent(body, r.Header.Get("Stripe-Signature"), h.secret)
	if err != nil {
		httpx.WriteBadRequest(w, r, "webhook_invalid_signature", "invalid signature", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	if err := h.handleEvent(r, &event); err != nil {
		slog.Error("webhook event processing failed", "event_type", event.Type, "error", err)
		httpx.WriteInternal(w, r, "webhook_processing_failed", "processing error", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleEvent(r *http.Request, event *stripe.Event) error {
	switch event.Type {
	case "checkout.session.completed":
		return h.handleCheckoutCompleted(r, event)
	case "invoice.paid":
		return h.handleInvoicePaid(event)
	case "invoice.payment_failed":
		return h.handleInvoicePaymentFailed(event)
	case "customer.subscription.updated":
		return h.handleSubscriptionUpdated(event)
	case "customer.subscription.deleted":
		return h.handleSubscriptionDeleted(event)
	default:
		slog.Warn("unhandled webhook event type", "event_type", event.Type)
		return nil
	}
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleCheckoutCompleted(r *http.Request, event *stripe.Event) error {
	var sess stripe.CheckoutSession
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &sess); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal checkout session: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Info("checkout completed", "customer", sess.Customer.ID, "subscription", sess.Subscription.ID)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleInvoicePaid(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var inv stripe.Invoice
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &inv); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal invoice: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Info("invoice paid", "customer", inv.Customer.ID, "amount", inv.AmountPaid)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleInvoicePaymentFailed(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var inv stripe.Invoice
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &inv); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal invoice: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Warn("invoice payment failed", "customer", inv.Customer.ID)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleSubscriptionUpdated(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var sub stripe.Subscription
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &sub); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal subscription: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Info("subscription updated", "customer", sub.Customer.ID, "status", sub.Status)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleSubscriptionDeleted(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var sub stripe.Subscription
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &sub); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal subscription: %w", err)
	}

[truncated]
```

package billing

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestWebhookHandlerMethodNotAllowed(t *testing.T) {
	h := NewWebhookHandler("whsec_test", nil)
	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/webhooks/stripe", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()

	h.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusMethodNotAllowed {
		t.Fatalf("status=%d, want=%d", rec.Code, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
	}
	if got := rec.Header().Get("Allow"); got != http.MethodPost {
		t.Fatalf("allow=%q, want=%q", got, http.MethodPost)
	}
	if got := rec.Header().Get("X-Request-Id"); got == "" {
		t.Fatal("missing X-Request-Id")
	}
	var body map[string]any
	if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &body); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("unmarshal body: %v", err)
	}
	if body["code"] != "method_not_allowed" {
		t.Fatalf("code=%v, want method_not_allowed", body["code"])
	}
	if body["request_id"] == "" {
		t.Fatal("missing request_id in body")
	}
}

func TestWebhookHandlerInvalidSignatureReturnsJSON(t *testing.T) {
	h := NewWebhookHandler("whsec_test", nil)
	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/webhooks/stripe", strings.NewReader(`{"id":"evt_123"}`))
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()

	h.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {
		t.Fatalf("status=%d, want=%d", rec.Code, http.StatusBadRequest)
	}
	if got := rec.Header().Get("Content-Type"); got != "application/json; charset=utf-8" {
		t.Fatalf("content-type=%q", got)
	}
	var body map[string]any
	if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &body); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("unmarshal body: %v", err)
	}
	if body["code"] != "webhook_invalid_signature" {
		t.Fatalf("code=%v, want webhook_invalid_signature", body["code"])
	}
	if body["request_id"] == "" {
		t.Fatal("missing request_id in body")
	}
}

It validates signatures and accepts the core events, but it does not yet persist richer subscription state transitions back into the Nanoclaw account model.\n+\n+For production rollout, make webhook behavior explicit:\n+\n+1. map Stripe customer and subscription IDs to local account state\n+2. define plan/tier transitions on `checkout.session.completed` and `invoice.paid`\n+3. define downgrade or grace-period behavior on failed payment or deleted subscription\n+4. add idempotency and replay-safe event handling\n+5. monitor failed webhook deliveries in Stripe Workbench\n@@\n ## Recommended Validation Matrix\n@@\n 4. Safari shows Apple Pay on the Checkout page.\n 5. Safari loads hosted Billing Portal.\n-6. Brave or Chrome loads Checkout without UI regressions, even if Apple Pay is absent.\n+6. package webhook

import (
	"crypto/hmac"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82"
)

//
// Public constants
//

const (
	// DefaultTolerance indicates that signatures older than this will be rejected by ConstructEvent.
	DefaultTolerance time.Duration = 300 * time.Second
	// signingVersion represents the version of the signature we currently use.
	signingVersion string = "v1"
)

//
// Public variables
//

// This block represents the list of errors that could be raised when using the webhook package.
var (
	ErrInvalidHeader    = errors.New("webhook has invalid Stripe-Signature header")
	ErrNoValidSignature = errors.New("webhook had no valid signature")
	ErrNotSigned        = errors.New("webhook has no Stripe-Signature header")
	ErrTooOld           = errors.New("timestamp wasn't within tolerance")
)

//
// Public functions
//

// ComputeSignature computes a webhook signature using Stripe's v1 signing
// method.
//
// See https://stripe.com/docs/webhooks#signatures for more information.
func ComputeSignature(t time.Time, payload []byte, secret string) []byte {
	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
	mac.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%d", t.Unix())))
	mac.Write([]byte("."))
	mac.Write(payload)
	return mac.Sum(nil)
}

// ConstructEvent initializes an Event object from a JSON webhook payload, validating
// the Stripe-Signature header using the specified signing secret. Returns an error
// if the body or Stripe-Signature header provided are unreadable, if the
// signature doesn't match, or if the timestamp for the signature is older than
// DefaultTolerance.
//
// NOTE: Stripe will only send Webhook signing headers after you have retrieved
// your signing secret from the Stripe dashboard:
// https://dashboard.stripe.com/webhooks
//
// This will return an error if the event API version does not match the
// stripe.APIVersion constant.
func ConstructEvent(payload []byte, header string, secret string) (stripe.Event, error) {
	return ConstructEventWithTolerance(payload, header, secret, DefaultTolerance)
}

// ConstructEventIgnoringTolerance initializes an Event object from a JSON webhook
// payload, validating the Stripe-Signature header using the specified signing secret.
// Returns an error if the body or Stripe-Signature header provided are unreadable or
// if the signature doesn't match. Does not check the signature's timestamp.
//
// NOTE: Stripe will only send Webhook signing headers after you have retrieved
// your signing secret from the Stripe dashboard:
// https://dashboard.stripe.com/webhooks
//
// This will return an error if the event API version does not match the
// stripe.APIVersion constant.
func ConstructEventIgnoringTolerance(payload []byte, header string, secret string) (stripe.Event, error) {
	return constructEvent(payload, header, secret, ConstructEventOptions{IgnoreTolerance: true})
}

// ConstructEventWithTolerance initializes an Event object from a JSON webhook payload,
// validating the signature in the Stripe-Signature header using the specified signing
// secret and tolerance window. Returns an error if the body or Stripe-Signature header
// provided are unreadable, if the signature doesn't match, or if the timestamp
// for the signature is older than the specified tolerance.
//
// NOTE: Stripe will only send Webhook signing headers after you have retrieved

> I found the parser; now I’m checking Stripe’s own test helper in the local source to see why the generated header isn’t matching, instead of fighting it blindly in our tests.

## Assistant

```text
Command: /bin/bash -lc 'pkgdir=$(go list -f '"'{{.Dir}}' github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/webhook) && rg -n 'GenerateTestSignedPayload|UnsignedPayload' \""'$pkgdir" && sed -n '"'240,360p' \""'$pkgdir/client.go"'
Chunk ID: 7865f9
Wall time: 0.0000 seconds
Process exited with code 0
Original token count: 697
Output:
/Users/tmc/go/pkg/mod/github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82@v82.5.1/webhook/client.go:300:type UnsignedPayload struct {
/Users/tmc/go/pkg/mod/github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82@v82.5.1/webhook/client.go:308:	UnsignedPayload
/Users/tmc/go/pkg/mod/github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82@v82.5.1/webhook/client.go:314:func GenerateTestSignedPayload(options *UnsignedPayload) *SignedPayload {
/Users/tmc/go/pkg/mod/github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82@v82.5.1/webhook/client.go:315:	signedPayload := &SignedPayload{UnsignedPayload: *options}
	// Signed header looks like "t=1495999758,v1=ABC,v1=DEF,v0=GHI"
	pairs := strings.Split(header, ",")
	for _, pair := range pairs {
		parts := strings.Split(pair, "=")
		if len(parts) != 2 {
			return sh, ErrInvalidHeader
		}

		switch parts[0] {
		case "t":
			timestamp, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)
			if err != nil {
				return sh, ErrInvalidHeader
			}
			sh.timestamp = time.Unix(timestamp, 0)

		case signingVersion:
			sig, err := hex.DecodeString(parts[1])
			if err != nil {
				continue // Ignore invalid signatures
			}

			sh.signatures = append(sh.signatures, sig)

		default:
			continue // Ignore unknown parts of the header
		}
	}

	if len(sh.signatures) == 0 {
		return sh, ErrNoValidSignature
	}

	return sh, nil
}

func validatePayload(payload []byte, sigHeader string, secret string, tolerance time.Duration, enforceTolerance bool) error {

	header, err := parseSignatureHeader(sigHeader)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	expiredTimestamp := time.Since(header.timestamp) > tolerance
	if enforceTolerance && expiredTimestamp {
		return ErrTooOld
	}

	expectedSignature := ComputeSignature(header.timestamp, payload, secret)
	// Check all given v1 signatures, multiple signatures will be sent temporarily in the case of a rolled signature secret
	for _, sig := range header.signatures {
		if hmac.Equal(expectedSignature, sig) {
			return nil
		}
	}

	return ErrNoValidSignature
}

// For mocking webhook events
type UnsignedPayload struct {
	Payload   []byte
	Secret    string
	Timestamp time.Time
	Scheme    string
}

type SignedPayload struct {
	UnsignedPayload

	Signature []byte
	Header    string
}

func GenerateTestSignedPayload(options *UnsignedPayload) *SignedPayload {
	signedPayload := &SignedPayload{UnsignedPayload: *options}

	if signedPayload.Timestamp == (time.Time{}) {
		signedPayload.Timestamp = time.Now()
	}

	if signedPayload.Scheme == "" {
		signedPayload.Scheme = "v1"
	}

	signedPayload.Signature = ComputeSignature(signedPayload.Timestamp, signedPayload.Payload, signedPayload.Secret)
	signedPayload.Header = generateHeader(*signedPayload)

	return signedPayload
}

func generateHeader(p SignedPayload) string {
	return fmt.Sprintf("t=%d,%s=%s", p.Timestamp.Unix(), p.Scheme, hex.EncodeToString(p.Signature))
}
```

## Assistant (final_answer)

> I continued through the remaining payments gap and tightened the webhook and rollout side.
>
> What I added:
> - `.env` template at [.env.example](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/.env.example)
> - Live Stripe + Safari runbook at [stripe-apple-pay-e2e.md](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/docs/design/stripe-apple-pay-e2e.md)
> - New opt-in live CDP redirect test in [cdp_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/cdp_test.go):
>   - `TestCDP_AccountBillingHostedRedirects`
> - Stronger webhook tests in [webhook_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/webhook_test.go)
>
> What is now verified:
> - Real Stripe Checkout session creation from Nanoclaw
> - Real Stripe Billing Portal session creation from Nanoclaw
> - Safari shows Apple Pay on the hosted Stripe Checkout page
> - Stripe’s hosted payment-method domain is active for Apple Pay
> - Signed Stripe webhook requests are accepted by the local webhook handler
> - Malformed signed webhook payloads hit the processing-error path
> - The opt-in live CDP browser test passes against real Stripe-hosted redirects
>
> Verification run:
> - `go test ./cmd/ncd/billing ./cmd/ncd/auth ./cmd/ncd -run 'TestWebhookHandler|TestDevInterceptor|TestCreate(CheckoutSession|BillingPortalSession)'`
> - `npm --prefix cmd/ncd/ui run build`
> - `NCD_CDP_LIVE_STRIPE=1 STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=... STRIPE_PRICE_ID=... go test -v -tags cdp -run 'TestCDP_AccountBillingHostedRedirects$' ./cmd/ncd -cdp-url http://127.0.0.1:8090`
>
> Where it stands now:
> - The Stripe subscription flow is working end to end in test mode.
> - Apple Pay is confirmed on the real hosted Checkout surface in Safari.
> - The remaining meaningful production gap is not payments plumbing. It is account-state reconciliation:
>   - webhook events are validated and parsed
>   - but Nanoclaw still only logs those lifecycle events instead of persisting plan/tier/subscription state transitions
>
> So the next serious step is clear:
> - persist Stripe customer/subscription status into the local account model on webhook events
>
> If you want, I can do that next: define the minimal subscription state we should store, wire webhook updates into it, and then add tests for upgrade/cancel/payment-failed transitions.

package db

import (
	"context"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"time"

	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
)

// PostgresStore provides PostgreSQL-backed persistence for users, sessions,
// API keys, and usage records.
type PostgresStore struct {
	pool *pgxpool.Pool
}

func newPostgresStore(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (Store, error) {
	pool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, databaseURL)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("connect to postgres: %w", err)
	}
	if err := pool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
		pool.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping postgres: %w", err)
	}
	s := &PostgresStore{pool: pool}
	if err := s.migrate(ctx); err != nil {
		pool.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("run migrations: %w", err)
	}
	return s, nil
}

// Close shuts down the connection pool.
func (s *PostgresStore) Close() {
	s.pool.Close()
}

// PoolStats returns a snapshot of pgxpool connection pool metrics.
func (s *PostgresStore) PoolStats() PoolStats {
	st := s.pool.Stat()
	return PoolStats{
		TotalConns:              st.TotalConns(),
		IdleConns:               st.IdleConns(),
		AcquiredConns:           st.AcquiredConns(),
		ConstructingConns:       st.ConstructingConns(),
		MaxConns:                st.MaxConns(),
		AcquireCount:            st.AcquireCount(),
		EmptyAcquireCount:       st.EmptyAcquireCount(),
		CanceledAcquireCount:    st.CanceledAcquireCount(),
		NewConnsCount:           st.NewConnsCount(),
		MaxLifetimeDestroyCount: st.MaxLifetimeDestroyCount(),
		MaxIdleDestroyCount:     st.MaxIdleDestroyCount(),
		AcquireDurationMs:       float64(st.AcquireDuration().Milliseconds()),
	}
}

// User represents a nanoclaw user.
type User struct {
	ID                  string
	Email               string
	GoogleSub           string
	Name                string
	PictureURL          string
	StripeCustomerID    string
	CreatedAt           time.Time
	Suspended           bool
	SuspendReason       string
	FreeTierUsedSeconds int64
}

// Session represents an authenticated session.
type Session struct {
	ID        string
	TokenHash string
	UserID    string
	CreatedAt time.Time
	ExpiresAt time.Time
	LastUsed  time.Time
	IPAddress string
	UserAgent string
}

// APIKey represents a stored API key.
type APIKey struct {
	ID        string
	UserID    string
	KeyPrefix string
	KeyHash   string
	Name      string
	Scopes    []string
	CreatedAt time.Time
	LastUsed  *time.Time
	ExpiresAt *time.Time
}

// UsageRecord represents a single usage measurement.
type UsageRecord struct {
	ID         int64
	UserID     string
	AgentID    string
	Metric     string
	Value      int64
	RecordedAt time.Time
}

// UpsertUserByGoogleSub creates or updates a user by Google subject ID.
// Returns the user and whether it was newly created.
func (s *PostgresStore) UpsertUserByGoogleSub(ctx context.Context, googleSub, email, name, pictureURL string) (*User, bool, error) {
	return s.UpsertUserByExternalSubject(ctx, "google", googleSub, email, name, pictureURL)
}

package db

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/json"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"time"

	_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
)

type SQLiteStore struct {
	db *sql.DB
}

func newSQLiteStore(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (Store, error) {
	dsn := sqliteDSN(databaseURL)
	if strings.HasPrefix(dsn, "/") {
		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dsn), 0o755); err != nil {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create sqlite dir: %w", err)
		}
	}

	sqlDB, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite: %w", err)
	}
	if sqliteInMemoryDSN(dsn) {
		// In-memory SQLite is per-connection by default. Keep a single
		// connection so schema and data remain visible across all queries.
		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(1)
		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(1)
	}
	if _, err := sqlDB.ExecContext(ctx, `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`); err != nil {
		sqlDB.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("set sqlite wal mode: %w", err)
	}
	if _, err := sqlDB.ExecContext(ctx, `PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON`); err != nil {
		sqlDB.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("enable sqlite foreign keys: %w", err)
	}

	s := &SQLiteStore{db: sqlDB}
	if err := s.migrate(ctx); err != nil {
		sqlDB.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("run sqlite migrations: %w", err)
	}
	return s, nil
}

func sqliteDSN(databaseURL string) string {
	v := strings.TrimSpace(databaseURL)
	switch {
	case strings.HasPrefix(v, "sqlite:///"):
		return strings.TrimPrefix(v, "sqlite://")
	case strings.HasPrefix(v, "sqlite://"):
		return strings.TrimPrefix(v, "sqlite://")
	case strings.HasPrefix(v, "file:"):
		return v
	default:
		return v
	}
}

func sqliteInMemoryDSN(dsn string) bool {
	v := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(dsn))
	return v == ":memory:" ||
		strings.HasPrefix(v, "file::memory:") ||
		strings.Contains(v, "mode=memory")
}

func sqliteTS(t time.Time) string {
	return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
}

func parseSQLiteTS(v string) (time.Time, error) {
	if v == "" {
		return time.Time{}, nil
	}
	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, v)
	if err == nil {
		return t, nil
	}
	return time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v)
}

func (s *SQLiteStore) migrate(ctx context.Context) error {
	stmts := []string{
		`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
			id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
			email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
			google_sub TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
			name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
			picture_url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
			stripe_id TEXT,
			created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			suspended INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
			suspend_reason TEXT,
			free_tier_used_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
		)`,
		`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
			id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
			token_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
			user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
			created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			expires_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			last_used TEXT,
			ip_address TEXT,
			user_agent TEXT,
			FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
		)`,
		`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_token_hash ON sessions(token_hash)`,
		`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_user_id ON sessions(user_id)`,
		`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (
			user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
			provider TEXT NOT NULL,
			provider_sub TEXT NOT NULL,
			email_at_link TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
			linked_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			last_sign_in_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			PRIMARY KEY (provider, provider_sub),
			FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
		)`,
		`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_user_identities_user_provider ON user_identities(user_id, provider)`,
		`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_user_identities_user_id ON user_identities(user_id)`,
		`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO user_identities (user_id, provider, provider_sub, email_at_link, linked_at, last_sign_in_at)
		SELECT id, 'google', google_sub, email, created_at, created_at
		FROM users
		WHERE google_sub <> '' AND google_sub NOT LIKE 'apple:%'`,

// NanoclawControllerService provides user-facing endpoints for

// Authentication uses Google OAuth exclusively.
service NanoclawControllerService {
  // GetAccount returns the current user's account details.
  rpc GetAccount(GetAccountRequest) returns (GetAccountResponse);
  // UpdateAccount updates the current user's account details.
  rpc UpdateAccount(UpdateAccountRequest) returns (UpdateAccountResponse);

  // StartDeviceAuth initiates a Google OAuth device flow for CLI login.
  rpc StartDeviceAuth(StartDeviceAuthRequest) returns (StartDeviceAuthResponse);
  // PollDeviceAuth polls for device authorization completion.
  rpc PollDeviceAuth(PollDeviceAuthRequest) returns (PollDeviceAuthResponse);

  // CreateAPIKey creates a new API key for programmatic access.
  rpc CreateAPIKey(CreateAPIKeyRequest) returns (CreateAPIKeyResponse);
  // ListAPIKeys lists all API keys for the current user.
  rpc ListAPIKeys(ListAPIKeysRequest) returns (ListAPIKeysResponse);
  // RevokeAPIKey revokes an API key.
  rpc RevokeAPIKey(RevokeAPIKeyRequest) returns (RevokeAPIKeyResponse);

  // CreateCheckoutSession creates a Stripe checkout session.
  rpc CreateCheckoutSession(CreateCheckoutSessionRequest) returns (CreateCheckoutSessionResponse);
  // CreateBillingPortalSession creates a Stripe billing portal session.
  rpc CreateBillingPortalSession(CreateBillingPortalSessionRequest) returns (CreateBillingPortalSessionResponse);
  // GetSubscription returns the user's subscription details.
  rpc GetSubscription(GetSubscriptionRequest) returns (GetSubscriptionResponse);
  // GetUsageSummary returns usage metrics for the current billing period.
  rpc GetUsageSummary(GetUsageSummaryRequest) returns (GetUsageSummaryResponse);

  // ListPaymentMethods returns the user's payment methods.
  rpc ListPaymentMethods(ListPaymentMethodsRequest) returns (ListPaymentMethodsResponse);

  // Logout invalidates the current session.
  rpc Logout(LogoutRequest) returns (LogoutResponse);
  // ListSessions lists all active sessions for the current user.
  rpc ListSessions(ListSessionsRequest) returns (ListSessionsResponse);
  // RevokeSession revokes a specific session.
  rpc RevokeSession(RevokeSessionRequest) returns (RevokeSessionResponse);
}

// Account

message GetAccountRequest {}

message GetAccountResponse {
  string user_id = 1;
  string email = 2;
  string name = 3;
  string picture_url = 4;
  string tier = 5;
  int64 free_tier_remaining_seconds = 6;
  bool has_payment_method = 7;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 8;
}

message UpdateAccountRequest {
  string name = 1;
}

message UpdateAccountResponse {
  GetAccountResponse account = 1;
}

// Device auth (CLI login)

message StartDeviceAuthRequest {}

message StartDeviceAuthResponse {
  string device_code = 1;
  string user_code = 2;
  string verification_uri = 3;
  string verification_uri_complete = 4;
  int32 expires_in_seconds = 5;
  int32 poll_interval_seconds = 6;
}

message PollDeviceAuthRequest {
  string device_code = 1;
}

message PollDeviceAuthResponse {
  bool authorized = 1;
  string session_token = 2;
  string user_id = 3;
  string email = 4;
}

// API keys

message CreateAPIKeyRequest {
  string name = 1;
  repeated string scopes = 2;
}

message CreateAPIKeyResponse {
  string api_key = 1;
  string key_id = 2;
  string key_prefix = 3;
}

message ListAPIKeysRequest {}

message APIKeyInfo {
  string id = 1;
  string name = 2;
  string key_prefix = 3;
  repeated string scopes = 4;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 5;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp last_used = 6;
}

message ListAPIKeysResponse {

[truncated]
```

syntax = "proto3";

package ncd.account.v1;

option go_package = "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/account/v1;ncdaccountv1";

import "google/protobuf/timestamp.proto";

// AccountService provides account, session, API key, and subscription views.
service AccountService {
  rpc GetAccount(GetAccountRequest) returns (Account);
  rpc GetCurrentAccount(GetCurrentAccountRequest) returns (Account);

  rpc ListSessions(ListSessionsRequest) returns (ListSessionsResponse);
  rpc RevokeSession(RevokeSessionRequest) returns (RevokeSessionResponse);

  rpc ListAPIKeys(ListAPIKeysRequest) returns (ListAPIKeysResponse);
  rpc CreateAPIKey(CreateAPIKeyRequest) returns (CreateAPIKeyResponse);
  rpc RevokeAPIKey(RevokeAPIKeyRequest) returns (RevokeAPIKeyResponse);

  rpc GetSubscription(GetSubscriptionRequest) returns (Subscription);

  // Account deletion (required for App Store compliance).
  // DeleteAccount schedules deletion after a 30-day grace period.
  // Pass immediate_purge=true for GDPR right-to-erasure requests.
  rpc DeleteAccount(DeleteAccountRequest) returns (DeleteAccountResponse);
  rpc CancelAccountDeletion(CancelAccountDeletionRequest) returns (CancelAccountDeletionResponse);
  rpc GetDeletionStatus(GetDeletionStatusRequest) returns (DeletionStatus);
}

enum AccountTier {
  ACCOUNT_TIER_UNSPECIFIED = 0;
  ACCOUNT_TIER_FREE = 1;
  ACCOUNT_TIER_PRO = 2;
  ACCOUNT_TIER_ENTERPRISE = 3;
}

enum SubscriptionStatus {
  SUBSCRIPTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED = 0;
  SUBSCRIPTION_STATUS_ACTIVE = 1;
  SUBSCRIPTION_STATUS_PAST_DUE = 2;
  SUBSCRIPTION_STATUS_CANCELED = 3;
  SUBSCRIPTION_STATUS_TRIALING = 4;
}

message Account {
  string user_id = 1;
  string email = 2;
  AccountTier tier = 3;
  bool suspended = 4;
  string suspension_reason = 5;
  int64 free_tier_remaining = 6;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 7;
}

message Session {
  string token = 1;
  string device_name = 2;
  string ip_address = 3;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 4;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp expires_at = 5;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp last_active_at = 6;
}

message APIKey {
  string key_id = 1;
  string key_prefix = 2;
  string name = 3;
  repeated string scopes = 4;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 5;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp expires_at = 6;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp last_used_at = 7;
}

message Subscription {
  string stripe_id = 1;
  AccountTier plan = 2;
  SubscriptionStatus status = 3;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp current_period_end = 4;
  int64 compute_seconds_remaining = 5;
}

message GetAccountRequest {
  string user_id = 1;
}

message GetCurrentAccountRequest {}

message ListSessionsRequest {
  string user_id = 1;
}

message ListSessionsResponse {
  repeated Session sessions = 1;
}

message RevokeSessionRequest {
  string user_id = 1;
  string token = 2;
  string reason = 3;
}

message RevokeSessionResponse {}

message ListAPIKeysRequest {
  string user_id = 1;
}

message ListAPIKeysResponse {
  repeated APIKey api_keys = 1;
}

message CreateAPIKeyRequest {
  string user_id = 1;
  APIKey api_key = 2;
}

message CreateAPIKeyResponse {
  APIKey api_key = 1;
  string secret_key = 2; // Returned only at creation time.
}

message RevokeAPIKeyRequest {
  string user_id = 1;
  string key_id = 2;
  string reason = 3;
}

message RevokeAPIKeyResponse {}

message GetSubscriptionRequest {
  string user_id = 1;
}

// DeletionState describes the lifecycle of an account deletion request.
enum DeletionState {
  DELETION_STATE_UNSPECIFIED = 0;
  DELETION_STATE_SCHEDULED   = 1; // Grace period running; cancellable.
  DELETION_STATE_PURGING     = 2; // Final purge in progress.
  DELETION_STATE_COMPLETED   = 3; // Account and data fully removed.
  DELETION_STATE_CANCELLED   = 4; // Cancelled during grace period.
}

Output:
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"strings"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
)

const (
	pricingTierFree       = "free"
	pricingTierPro        = "pro"
	pricingTierEnterprise = "enterprise"
)

func normalizePricingTier(raw string) string {
	switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(raw)) {
	case pricingTierEnterprise:
		return pricingTierEnterprise
	case pricingTierPro, "standard", "paid":
		return pricingTierPro
	default:
		return pricingTierFree
	}
}

func pricingTierRank(tier string) int {
	switch normalizePricingTier(tier) {
	case pricingTierEnterprise:
		return 3
	case pricingTierPro:
		return 2
	default:
		return 1
	}
}

func requiredPricingTierForResources(vcpus, memoryMB int32) string {
	if vcpus <= 0 {
		vcpus = 1
	}
	if memoryMB <= 0 {
		memoryMB = 2048
	}
	switch {
	case vcpus <= 2 && memoryMB <= 4096:
		return pricingTierFree
	case vcpus <= 8 && memoryMB <= 16384:
		return pricingTierPro
	default:
		return pricingTierEnterprise
	}
}

func validatePricingTierResources(pricingTier string, vcpus, memoryMB int32) error {
	required := requiredPricingTierForResources(vcpus, memoryMB)
	if pricingTierRank(pricingTier) >= pricingTierRank(required) {
		return nil
	}
	return fmt.Errorf("%d vCPU / %d MB requires %s plan", vcpus, memoryMB, required)
}

func tierForUser(user *db.User) string {
	if user == nil {
		return pricingTierFree
	}
	if isEnterpriseUser(user) {
		return pricingTierEnterprise
	}
	if strings.TrimSpace(user.StripeCustomerID) != "" {
		return pricingTierPro
	}
	return pricingTierFree
}

func pricingTierFromContextUser(user *db.User) string {
	return normalizePricingTier(tierForUser(user))
}

func isEnterpriseUser(user *db.User) bool {
	if user == nil {
		return false
	}
	allowlist := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NCD_ENTERPRISE_USERS"))
	if allowlist == "" {
		return false
	}
	id := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(user.ID))
	email := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(user.Email))
	for _, raw := range strings.Split(allowlist, ",") {
		v := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(raw))
		if v == "" {
			continue
		}
		if v == id || v == email {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}
```

func (s *nanoclawServer) GetSubscription(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.GetSubscriptionRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.GetSubscriptionResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.GetSubscriptionResponse{
		Tier:   tierForUser(user),
		Status: "active",
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) GetUsageSummary(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.GetUsageSummaryRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.GetUsageSummaryResponse], error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("database not initialized"))
	}
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}

	since := time.Now().AddDate(0, -1, 0)
	compute, storage, err := s.store.UsageSummary(ctx, user.ID, since)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	remaining := int64(freeTierLimitSeconds) - user.FreeTierUsedSeconds
	if remaining < 0 {
		remaining = 0
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.GetUsageSummaryResponse{
		ComputeSeconds:           compute,
		StorageBytes:             storage,
		FreeTierRemainingSeconds: remaining,
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) ListPaymentMethods(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}
	if s.billing == nil || user.StripeCustomerID == "" {
		return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsResponse{}), nil
	}

	methods, err := s.billing.ListPaymentMethods(user.StripeCustomerID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	var pms []*ncdv1.PaymentMethod
	for _, m := range methods {
		pm := &ncdv1.PaymentMethod{Id: m.ID}
```

package db

import (
	"context"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"time"

	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
)

// PostgresStore provides PostgreSQL-backed persistence for users, sessions,
// API keys, and usage records.
type PostgresStore struct {
	pool *pgxpool.Pool
}

func newPostgresStore(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (Store, error) {
	pool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, databaseURL)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("connect to postgres: %w", err)
	}
	if err := pool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
		pool.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping postgres: %w", err)
	}
	s := &PostgresStore{pool: pool}
	if err := s.migrate(ctx); err != nil {
		pool.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("run migrations: %w", err)
	}
	return s, nil
}

// Close shuts down the connection pool.
func (s *PostgresStore) Close() {
	s.pool.Close()
}

// PoolStats returns a snapshot of pgxpool connection pool metrics.
func (s *PostgresStore) PoolStats() PoolStats {
	st := s.pool.Stat()
	return PoolStats{
		TotalConns:              st.TotalConns(),
		IdleConns:               st.IdleConns(),
		AcquiredConns:           st.AcquiredConns(),
		ConstructingConns:       st.ConstructingConns(),
		MaxConns:                st.MaxConns(),
		AcquireCount:            st.AcquireCount(),
		EmptyAcquireCount:       st.EmptyAcquireCount(),
		CanceledAcquireCount:    st.CanceledAcquireCount(),
		NewConnsCount:           st.NewConnsCount(),
		MaxLifetimeDestroyCount: st.MaxLifetimeDestroyCount(),
		MaxIdleDestroyCount:     st.MaxIdleDestroyCount(),
		AcquireDurationMs:       float64(st.AcquireDuration().Milliseconds()),
	}
}

// User represents a nanoclaw user.
type User struct {
	ID                  string
	Email               string
	GoogleSub           string
	Name                string
	PictureURL          string
	StripeCustomerID    string

// Session represents an authenticated session.
type Session struct {
	ID        string
	TokenHash string
	UserID    string
	CreatedAt time.Time
	ExpiresAt time.Time
	LastUsed  time.Time
	IPAddress string
	UserAgent string
}

// APIKey represents a stored API key.
type APIKey struct {
	ID        string
	UserID    string
	KeyPrefix string
	KeyHash   string
	Name      string
	Scopes    []string
	CreatedAt time.Time
	LastUsed  *time.Time
	ExpiresAt *time.Time
}

// UsageRecord represents a single usage measurement.
type UsageRecord struct {
	ID         int64
	UserID     string
	AgentID    string
	Metric     string
	Value      int64
	RecordedAt time.Time
}

package billing

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82"
	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/webhook"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/internal/httpx"
)

// WebhookHandler processes Stripe webhook events.
type WebhookHandler struct {
	secret string
	store  db.Store
}

// NewWebhookHandler creates a webhook handler with the given signing secret.
func NewWebhookHandler(secret string, store db.Store) *WebhookHandler {
	return &WebhookHandler{secret: secret, store: store}
}

// ServeHTTP handles incoming Stripe webhook requests.
func (h *WebhookHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
		httpx.WriteMethodNotAllowed(w, r, http.MethodPost)
		return
	}

	body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r.Body, 1<<16))
	if err != nil {
		httpx.WriteBadRequest(w, r, "webhook_read_body_failed", "invalid request body", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	event, err := webhook.ConstructEvent(body, r.Header.Get("Stripe-Signature"), h.secret)
	if err != nil {
		httpx.WriteBadRequest(w, r, "webhook_invalid_signature", "invalid signature", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	if err := h.handleEvent(r, &event); err != nil {
		slog.Error("webhook event processing failed", "event_type", event.Type, "error", err)
		httpx.WriteInternal(w, r, "webhook_processing_failed", "processing error", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleEvent(r *http.Request, event *stripe.Event) error {
	switch event.Type {
	case "checkout.session.completed":
		return h.handleCheckoutCompleted(r, event)
	case "invoice.paid":
		return h.handleInvoicePaid(event)
	case "invoice.payment_failed":
		return h.handleInvoicePaymentFailed(event)
	case "customer.subscription.updated":
		return h.handleSubscriptionUpdated(event)
	case "customer.subscription.deleted":
		return h.handleSubscriptionDeleted(event)
	default:
		slog.Warn("unhandled webhook event type", "event_type", event.Type)
		return nil
	}
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleCheckoutCompleted(r *http.Request, event *stripe.Event) error {
	var sess stripe.CheckoutSession
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &sess); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal checkout session: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Info("checkout completed", "customer", sess.Customer.ID, "subscription", sess.Subscription.ID)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleInvoicePaid(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var inv stripe.Invoice
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &inv); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal invoice: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Info("invoice paid", "customer", inv.Customer.ID, "amount", inv.AmountPaid)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleInvoicePaymentFailed(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var inv stripe.Invoice
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &inv); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal invoice: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Warn("invoice payment failed", "customer", inv.Customer.ID)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleSubscriptionUpdated(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var sub stripe.Subscription
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &sub); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal subscription: %w", err)
	}
	slog.Info("subscription updated", "customer", sub.Customer.ID, "status", sub.Status)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleSubscriptionDeleted(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var sub stripe.Subscription
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &sub); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal subscription: %w", err)
	}

[truncated]

Original token count: 534
Output:
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"strings"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
)

const (
	pricingTierFree       = "free"
	pricingTierPro        = "pro"
	pricingTierEnterprise = "enterprise"
)

func normalizePricingTier(raw string) string {
	switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(raw)) {
	case pricingTierEnterprise:
		return pricingTierEnterprise
	case pricingTierPro, "standard", "paid":
		return pricingTierPro
	default:
		return pricingTierFree
	}
}

func pricingTierRank(tier string) int {
	switch normalizePricingTier(tier) {
	case pricingTierEnterprise:
		return 3
	case pricingTierPro:
		return 2
	default:
		return 1
	}
}

func requiredPricingTierForResources(vcpus, memoryMB int32) string {
	if vcpus <= 0 {
		vcpus = 1
	}
	if memoryMB <= 0 {
		memoryMB = 2048
	}
	switch {
	case vcpus <= 2 && memoryMB <= 4096:
		return pricingTierFree
	case vcpus <= 8 && memoryMB <= 16384:
		return pricingTierPro
	default:
		return pricingTierEnterprise
	}
}

func validatePricingTierResources(pricingTier string, vcpus, memoryMB int32) error {
	required := requiredPricingTierForResources(vcpus, memoryMB)
	if pricingTierRank(pricingTier) >= pricingTierRank(required) {
		return nil
	}
	return fmt.Errorf("%d vCPU / %d MB requires %s plan", vcpus, memoryMB, required)
}

func tierForUser(user *db.User) string {
	if user == nil {
		return pricingTierFree
	}
	if isEnterpriseUser(user) {
		return pricingTierEnterprise
	}
	if strings.TrimSpace(user.StripeCustomerID) != "" {
		return pricingTierPro
	}
	return pricingTierFree
}

func pricingTierFromContextUser(user *db.User) string {
	return normalizePricingTier(tierForUser(user))
}

func isEnterpriseUser(user *db.User) bool {
	if user == nil {
		return false
	}
	allowlist := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NCD_ENTERPRISE_USERS"))
	if allowlist == "" {
		return false
	}
	id := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(user.ID))
	email := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(user.Email))
	for _, raw := range strings.Split(allowlist, ",") {
		v := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(raw))
		if v == "" {
			continue
		}
		if v == id || v == email {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}
```

func (s *nanoclawServer) GetSubscription(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.GetSubscriptionRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.GetSubscriptionResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.GetSubscriptionResponse{
		Tier:   tierForUser(user),
		Status: "active",
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) GetUsageSummary(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.GetUsageSummaryRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.GetUsageSummaryResponse], error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("database not initialized"))
	}
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}

	since := time.Now().AddDate(0, -1, 0)
	compute, storage, err := s.store.UsageSummary(ctx, user.ID, since)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	remaining := int64(freeTierLimitSeconds) - user.FreeTierUsedSeconds
	if remaining < 0 {
		remaining = 0
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.GetUsageSummaryResponse{
		ComputeSeconds:           compute,
		StorageBytes:             storage,
		FreeTierRemainingSeconds: remaining,
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) ListPaymentMethods(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, nil)
	}
	if s.billing == nil || user.StripeCustomerID == "" {
		return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.ListPaymentMethodsResponse{}), nil
	}

	methods, err := s.billing.ListPaymentMethods(user.StripeCustomerID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, err)
	}

	var pms []*ncdv1.PaymentMethod
	for _, m := range methods {
		pm := &ncdv1.PaymentMethod{Id: m.ID}
		if m.Card != nil {
			pm.Last4 = m.Card.Last4
			pm.Brand = string(m.Card.Brand)
			pm.ExpMonth = int32(m.Card.ExpMonth)
			pm.ExpYear = int32(m.Card.ExpYear)
		}
		pms = append(pms, pm)

// GetUser returns a user by ID.
func (s *PostgresStore) GetUser(ctx context.Context, id string) (*User, error) {
	var u User
	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
		`SELECT id, email, google_sub, name, picture_url, COALESCE(stripe_id, ''),
			created_at, suspended, COALESCE(suspend_reason, ''), free_tier_used_seconds
		FROM users WHERE id = $1`, id,
	).Scan(&u.ID, &u.Email, &u.GoogleSub, &u.Name, &u.PictureURL,
		&u.StripeCustomerID, &u.CreatedAt, &u.Suspended, &u.SuspendReason,
		&u.FreeTierUsedSeconds)
	if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("user not found")
	}
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get user: %w", err)
	}
	return &u, nil
}

// SetStripeCustomerID sets the Stripe customer ID for a user.
func (s *PostgresStore) SetStripeCustomerID(ctx context.Context, userID, stripeID string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
		`UPDATE users SET stripe_id = $1 WHERE id = $2`, stripeID, userID)
	return err
}

// CreateSession inserts a new session record.
func (s *PostgresStore) CreateSession(ctx context.Context, sess *Session) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
		`INSERT INTO sessions (id, token_hash, user_id, created_at, expires_at, ip_address, user_agent)
		VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)`,
		sess.ID, sess.TokenHash, sess.UserID, sess.CreatedAt, sess.ExpiresAt,
		sess.IPAddress, sess.UserAgent)
	return err
}

// GetSessionByTokenHash looks up a session by its token hash.
func (s *PostgresStore) GetSessionByTokenHash(ctx context.Context, tokenHash string) (*Session, error) {
	var sess Session
	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
		`SELECT id, token_hash, user_id, created_at, expires_at,
			COALESCE(last_used, created_at), COALESCE(ip_address, ''), COALESCE(user_agent, '')
		FROM sessions WHERE token_hash = $1 AND expires_at > now()`, tokenHash,
	).Scan(&sess.ID, &sess.TokenHash, &sess.UserID, &sess.CreatedAt,
		&sess.ExpiresAt, &sess.LastUsed, &sess.IPAddress, &sess.UserAgent)
	if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session not found or expired")
	}
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get session: %w", err)
	}
	return &sess, nil
}

// TouchSession updates the last_used timestamp.
func (s *PostgresStore) TouchSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
		`UPDATE sessions SET last_used = now() WHERE id = $1`, sessionID)
	return err
}

// DeleteSession removes a session.
func (s *PostgresStore) DeleteSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = $1`, sessionID)
	return err
}

// DeleteSessionByTokenHash removes a session by its token hash.
func (s *PostgresStore) DeleteSessionByTokenHash(ctx context.Context, tokenHash string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash)
	return err
}

// ListSessionsByUser returns all active sessions for a user.
func (s *PostgresStore) ListSessionsByUser(ctx context.Context, userID string) ([]*Session, error) {
	rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
		`SELECT id, token_hash, user_id, created_at, expires_at,
			COALESCE(last_used, created_at), COALESCE(ip_address, ''), COALESCE(user_agent, '')
		FROM sessions WHERE user_id = $1 AND expires_at > now()
		ORDER BY created_at DESC`, userID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	defer rows.Close()

	var sessions []*Session
	for rows.Next() {
		var sess Session
		if err := rows.Scan(&sess.ID, &sess.TokenHash, &sess.UserID, &sess.CreatedAt,
			&sess.ExpiresAt, &sess.LastUsed, &sess.IPAddress, &sess.UserAgent); err != nil {

```

func (s *SQLiteStore) GetUser(ctx context.Context, id string) (*User, error) {
	var (
		u         User
		createdAt string
		suspended int
	)
	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
		`SELECT id, email, google_sub, name, picture_url, COALESCE(stripe_id,''), created_at,
			COALESCE(suspended,0), COALESCE(suspend_reason,''), COALESCE(free_tier_used_seconds,0)
		FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id,
	).Scan(&u.ID, &u.Email, &u.GoogleSub, &u.Name, &u.PictureURL, &u.StripeCustomerID,
		&createdAt, &suspended, &u.SuspendReason, &u.FreeTierUsedSeconds)
	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("user not found")
	}
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get user: %w", err)
	}
	t, err := parseSQLiteTS(createdAt)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse user created_at: %w", err)
	}
	u.CreatedAt = t
	u.Suspended = suspended != 0
	return &u, nil
}

syntax = "proto3";

package ncd.v1;

option go_package = "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/v1;ncdv1";

import "google/protobuf/timestamp.proto";

// NanoclawControllerService provides user-facing endpoints for
// authentication, account management, billing, and session management.
// Authentication uses Google OAuth exclusively.
service NanoclawControllerService {
  // GetAccount returns the current user's account details.
  rpc GetAccount(GetAccountRequest) returns (GetAccountResponse);
  // UpdateAccount updates the current user's account details.
  rpc UpdateAccount(UpdateAccountRequest) returns (UpdateAccountResponse);

  // StartDeviceAuth initiates a Google OAuth device flow for CLI login.
  rpc StartDeviceAuth(StartDeviceAuthRequest) returns (StartDeviceAuthResponse);
  // PollDeviceAuth polls for device authorization completion.
  rpc PollDeviceAuth(PollDeviceAuthRequest) returns (PollDeviceAuthResponse);

  // CreateAPIKey creates a new API key for programmatic access.
  rpc CreateAPIKey(CreateAPIKeyRequest) returns (CreateAPIKeyResponse);
  // ListAPIKeys lists all API keys for the current user.
  rpc ListAPIKeys(ListAPIKeysRequest) returns (ListAPIKeysResponse);
  // RevokeAPIKey revokes an API key.
  rpc RevokeAPIKey(RevokeAPIKeyRequest) returns (RevokeAPIKeyResponse);

  // CreateCheckoutSession creates a Stripe checkout session.
  rpc CreateCheckoutSession(CreateCheckoutSessionRequest) returns (CreateCheckoutSessionResponse);
  // CreateBillingPortalSession creates a Stripe billing portal session.
  rpc CreateBillingPortalSession(CreateBillingPortalSessionRequest) returns (CreateBillingPortalSessionResponse);
  // GetSubscription returns the user's subscription details.
  rpc GetSubscription(GetSubscriptionRequest) returns (GetSubscriptionResponse);
  // GetUsageSummary returns usage metrics for the current billing period.
  rpc GetUsageSummary(GetUsageSummaryRequest) returns (GetUsageSummaryResponse);

  // ListPaymentMethods returns the user's payment methods.
  rpc ListPaymentMethods(ListPaymentMethodsRequest) returns (ListPaymentMethodsResponse);

  // Logout invalidates the current session.
  rpc Logout(LogoutRequest) returns (LogoutResponse);
  // ListSessions lists all active sessions for the current user.
  rpc ListSessions(ListSessionsRequest) returns (ListSessionsResponse);
  // RevokeSession revokes a specific session.
  rpc RevokeSession(RevokeSessionRequest) returns (RevokeSessionResponse);
}

// Account

message GetAccountRequest {}

message GetAccountResponse {
  string user_id = 1;
  string email = 2;
  string name = 3;
  string picture_url = 4;
  string tier = 5;
  int64 free_tier_remaining_seconds = 6;
  bool has_payment_method = 7;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 8;
}

message UpdateAccountRequest {
  string name = 1;
}

message UpdateAccountResponse {
  GetAccountResponse account = 1;
}

// Device auth (CLI login)

message StartDeviceAuthRequest {}

message StartDeviceAuthResponse {
  string device_code = 1;
  string user_code = 2;
  string verification_uri = 3;
  string verification_uri_complete = 4;
  int32 expires_in_seconds = 5;
  int32 poll_interval_seconds = 6;
}

message PollDeviceAuthRequest {
  string device_code = 1;
}

message PollDeviceAuthResponse {
  bool authorized = 1;
  string session_token = 2;
  string user_id = 3;
  string email = 4;
}

// API keys

message CreateAPIKeyRequest {
  string name = 1;
  repeated string scopes = 2;
}

message CreateAPIKeyResponse {
  string api_key = 1;
  string key_id = 2;
  string key_prefix = 3;
}

message ListAPIKeysRequest {}

message APIKeyInfo {
  string id = 1;
  string name = 2;
  string key_prefix = 3;
  repeated string scopes = 4;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 5;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp last_used = 6;
}

message ListAPIKeysResponse {

[truncated]

package billing

import (
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/webhook"
)

func TestWebhookHandlerMethodNotAllowed(t *testing.T) {
	h := NewWebhookHandler("whsec_test", nil)
	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/webhooks/stripe", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()

	h.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusMethodNotAllowed {
		t.Fatalf("status=%d, want=%d", rec.Code, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
	}
	if got := rec.Header().Get("Allow"); got != http.MethodPost {
		t.Fatalf("allow=%q, want=%q", got, http.MethodPost)
	}
	if got := rec.Header().Get("X-Request-Id"); got == "" {
		t.Fatal("missing X-Request-Id")
	}
	var body map[string]any
	if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &body); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("unmarshal body: %v", err)
	}
	if body["code"] != "method_not_allowed" {
		t.Fatalf("code=%v, want method_not_allowed", body["code"])
	}
	if body["request_id"] == "" {
		t.Fatal("missing request_id in body")
	}
}

func TestWebhookHandlerInvalidSignatureReturnsJSON(t *testing.T) {
	h := NewWebhookHandler("whsec_test", nil)
	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/webhooks/stripe", strings.NewReader(`{"id":"evt_123"}`))
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()

	h.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {
		t.Fatalf("status=%d, want=%d", rec.Code, http.StatusBadRequest)
	}
	if got := rec.Header().Get("Content-Type"); got != "application/json; charset=utf-8" {
		t.Fatalf("content-type=%q", got)
	}
	var body map[string]any
	if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &body); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("unmarshal body: %v", err)
	}
	if body["code"] != "webhook_invalid_signature" {
		t.Fatalf("code=%v, want webhook_invalid_signature", body["code"])
	}
	if body["request_id"] == "" {
		t.Fatal("missing request_id in body")
	}
}

func TestWebhookHandlerCheckoutCompletedReturnsOK(t *testing.T) {
	h := NewWebhookHandler("whsec_test", nil)
	payload := []byte(`{
		"id":"evt_test_checkout_completed",
		"object":"event",
		"api_version":"2025-08-27.basil",
		"type":"checkout.session.completed",
		"data":{
			"object":{
				"id":"cs_test_123",
				"object":"checkout.session",
				"customer":"cus_test_123",
				"subscription":"sub_test_123"
			}
		}
	}`)
	header := signedStripeHeader(payload, "whsec_test")

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/webhooks/stripe", strings.NewReader(string(payload)))
	req.Header.Set("Stripe-Signature", header)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()

	h.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Fatalf("status=%d, want=%d body=%s", rec.Code, http.StatusOK, rec.Body.String())
	}
}

func TestWebhookHandlerProcessingErrorReturnsJSON(t *testing.T) {
	h := NewWebhookHandler("whsec_test", nil)
	payload := []byte(`{
		"id":"evt_test_checkout_completed_bad",
		"object":"event",
		"api_version":"2025-08-27.basil",
		"type":"checkout.session.completed",
		"data":{
			"object":{
				"id":123
			}
		}
	}`)
	header := signedStripeHeader(payload, "whsec_test")

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/webhooks/stripe", strings.NewReader(string(payload)))
	req.Header.Set("Stripe-Signature", header)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()

	h.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusInternalServerError {
		t.Fatalf("status=%d, want=%d", rec.Code, http.StatusInternalServerError)
	}
	var body map[string]any
	if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &body); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("unmarshal body: %v", err)
	}
	if body["code"] != "webhook_processing_failed" {
		t.Fatalf("code=%v, want webhook_processing_failed", body["code"])
	}
}

func signedStripeHeader(payload []byte, secret string) string {
	ts := time.Now().UTC()
	sig := webhook.ComputeSignature(ts, payload, secret)

func TestStartAgentEnforcesPricingTierResources(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	state := newDemoState()
	srv := &agentServer{state: state}

	_, err := srv.StartAgent(context.Background(), connect.NewRequest(&ncdagentv1.StartAgentRequest{
		Name:     "agent-resource-gate",
		Vcpus:    4,
		MemoryMb: 8192,
	}))
	if err == nil {
		t.Fatal("start agent error = nil, want pricing tier rejection")
	}
	if connect.CodeOf(err) != connect.CodeFailedPrecondition {
		t.Fatalf("start agent code = %v, want %v", connect.CodeOf(err), connect.CodeFailedPrecondition)
	}
}

func TestStartAgentAllowsProResourceProfile(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	state := newDemoState()
	srv := &agentServer{state: state}
	ctx := auth.ContextWithUser(context.Background(), &db.User{
		ID:               "pro-user",
		StripeCustomerID: "cus_test_pro",
	})

	resp, err := srv.StartAgent(ctx, connect.NewRequest(&ncdagentv1.StartAgentRequest{
		Name:     "agent-pro-resources",
		Vcpus:    4,
		MemoryMb: 8192,
	}))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("start agent: %v", err)
	}
	if resp.Msg.GetAgent().GetVcpus() != 4 || resp.Msg.GetAgent().GetMemoryMb() != 8192 {
		t.Fatalf("agent resources = %d vCPU / %d MB, want 4 vCPU / 8192 MB",
			resp.Msg.GetAgent().GetVcpus(), resp.Msg.GetAgent().GetMemoryMb())
	}
}
