func (s *nanoclawServer) Status(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[nanoclawv1.StatusRequest]) (*connect.Response[nanoclawv1.StatusResponse], error) {
	return connect.NewResponse(&nanoclawv1.StatusResponse{
		Status:      "ok",
		UptimeSince: timestamppb.New(s.startTime),
	}), nil
}

// NanoclawControllerService implementation.

func (s *nanoclawServer) GetAccount(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.GetAccountRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.GetAccountResponse], error) {
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, fmt.Errorf("not authenticated"))
	}

	remaining := int64(freeTierLimitSeconds) - user.FreeTierUsedSeconds
	if remaining < 0 {
		remaining = 0
	}

	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.GetAccountResponse{
		UserId:                   user.ID,
		Email:                    user.Email,
		Name:                     user.Name,
		PictureUrl:               user.PictureURL,
		Tier:                     tierForUser(user),
		FreeTierRemainingSeconds: remaining,
		HasPaymentMethod:         user.StripeCustomerID != "",
		CreatedAt:                timestamppb.New(user.CreatedAt),
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) UpdateAccount(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.UpdateAccountRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.UpdateAccountResponse], error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("database not initialized"))
	}
	user, ok := auth.UserFromContext(ctx)
	if !ok {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnauthenticated, fmt.Errorf("not authenticated"))
	}
	name := strings.TrimSpace(req.Msg.Name)
	if name == "" {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("name is required"))
	}
	if utf8.RuneCountInString(name) > 120 {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, fmt.Errorf("name is too long"))
	}
	if err := s.store.UpdateUserName(ctx, user.ID, name); err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, fmt.Errorf("update account: %w", err))
	}
	refreshed, err := s.store.GetUser(ctx, user.ID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeInternal, fmt.Errorf("get updated account: %w", err))
	}
	remaining := int64(freeTierLimitSeconds) - refreshed.FreeTierUsedSeconds
	if remaining < 0 {
		remaining = 0
	}
	return connect.NewResponse(&ncdv1.UpdateAccountResponse{
		Account: &ncdv1.GetAccountResponse{
			UserId:                   refreshed.ID,
			Email:                    refreshed.Email,
			Name:                     refreshed.Name,
			PictureUrl:               refreshed.PictureURL,
			Tier:                     tierForUser(refreshed),
			FreeTierRemainingSeconds: remaining,
			HasPaymentMethod:         refreshed.StripeCustomerID != "",
			CreatedAt:                timestamppb.New(refreshed.CreatedAt),
		},
	}), nil
}

func (s *nanoclawServer) StartDeviceAuth(ctx context.Context, req *connect.Request[ncdv1.StartDeviceAuthRequest]) (*connect.Response[ncdv1.StartDeviceAuthResponse], error) {
	if s.deviceFlowConfigErr != "" {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("%s", s.deviceFlowConfigErr))
	}
	if s.deviceFlow == nil {
		return nil, connect.NewError(connect.CodeUnavailable, fmt.Errorf("device flow not configured"))
	}
```

package db

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/json"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"time"

	_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
)

type SQLiteStore struct {
	db *sql.DB
}

func newSQLiteStore(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (Store, error) {
	dsn := sqliteDSN(databaseURL)
	if strings.HasPrefix(dsn, "/") {
		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dsn), 0o755); err != nil {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create sqlite dir: %w", err)
		}
	}

	sqlDB, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite: %w", err)
	}
	if sqliteInMemoryDSN(dsn) {
		// In-memory SQLite is per-connection by default. Keep a single
		// connection so schema and data remain visible across all queries.
		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(1)
		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(1)
	}
	if _, err := sqlDB.ExecContext(ctx, `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`); err != nil {
		sqlDB.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("set sqlite wal mode: %w", err)
	}
	if _, err := sqlDB.ExecContext(ctx, `PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON`); err != nil {
		sqlDB.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("enable sqlite foreign keys: %w", err)
	}

	s := &SQLiteStore{db: sqlDB}
	if err := s.migrate(ctx); err != nil {
		sqlDB.Close()
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("run sqlite migrations: %w", err)
	}
	return s, nil
}

func sqliteDSN(databaseURL string) string {
	v := strings.TrimSpace(databaseURL)
	switch {
	case strings.HasPrefix(v, "sqlite:///"):
		return strings.TrimPrefix(v, "sqlite://")
	case strings.HasPrefix(v, "sqlite://"):
		return strings.TrimPrefix(v, "sqlite://")
	case strings.HasPrefix(v, "file:"):
		return v
	default:
		return v
	}
}

func sqliteInMemoryDSN(dsn string) bool {
	v := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(dsn))
	return v == ":memory:" ||
		strings.HasPrefix(v, "file::memory:") ||
		strings.Contains(v, "mode=memory")
}

func sqliteTS(t time.Time) string {
	return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
}

func parseSQLiteTS(v string) (time.Time, error) {
	if v == "" {
		return time.Time{}, nil
	}
	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, v)
	if err == nil {
		return t, nil
	}
	return time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v)
}

func (s *SQLiteStore) migrate(ctx context.Context) error {
	stmts := []string{
		`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
			id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
			email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
			google_sub TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
			name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
			picture_url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
			stripe_id TEXT,
			created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			suspended INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
			suspend_reason TEXT,
			free_tier_used_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
		)`,
		`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
			id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
			token_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
			user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
			created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			expires_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			last_used TEXT,
			ip_address TEXT,
			user_agent TEXT,
			FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
		)`,
		`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_token_hash ON sessions(token_hash)`,
		`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_user_id ON sessions(user_id)`,
		`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (
			user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
			provider TEXT NOT NULL,
			provider_sub TEXT NOT NULL,
			email_at_link TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
			linked_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			last_sign_in_at TEXT NOT NULL,
			PRIMARY KEY (provider, provider_sub),
			FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

		`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_user_identities_user_id ON user_identities(user_id)`,
		`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO user_identities (user_id, provider, provider_sub, email_at_link, linked_at, last_sign_in_at)
		SELECT id, 'google', google_sub, email, created_at, created_at
		FROM users
		WHERE google_sub <> '' AND google_sub NOT LIKE 'apple:%'`,

package auth

import (
	"context"
	"net/http"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"connectrpc.com/connect"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
)

type contextKey string

const userContextKey contextKey = "authenticated_user"

// UserFromContext returns the authenticated user from the request context.
func UserFromContext(ctx context.Context) (*db.User, bool) {
	u, ok := ctx.Value(userContextKey).(*db.User)
	return u, ok
}

// ContextWithUser returns a new context with the given user set as the
// authenticated user. Useful for plain HTTP handlers that authenticate
// outside of the Connect-RPC interceptor chain.
func ContextWithUser(ctx context.Context, user *db.User) context.Context {
	return context.WithValue(ctx, userContextKey, user)
}

// SessionIDFromContext returns the session ID from the request context.
func SessionIDFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
	v, _ := ctx.Value(contextKey("session_id")).(string)
	return v
}

// TokenHashFromContext returns the token hash from the request context.
func TokenHashFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
	v, _ := ctx.Value(contextKey("token_hash")).(string)
	return v
}

// publicProcedures lists RPC procedures that don't require authentication.
var publicProcedures = map[string]bool{
	"/ncd.v1.NanoclawControllerService/StartDeviceAuth":           true,
	"/ncd.v1.NanoclawControllerService/PollDeviceAuth":            true,
	"/ncd.testflight.v1.TestFlightService/RegisterForEarlyAccess": true,
	"/ncd.testflight.v1.TestFlightService/GetRegistrationStatus":  true,
}

const devSessionToken = "ncd_dev_token"

type authInterceptorConfig struct {
	allowDevSession bool
	rateLimiter     *RateLimiter
}

type AuthInterceptorOption func(*authInterceptorConfig)

// WithDevSessionFallback allows the special dev session token to authenticate
// as a synthetic development user.
func WithDevSessionFallback() AuthInterceptorOption {
	return func(cfg *authInterceptorConfig) {
		cfg.allowDevSession = true
	}
}

// WithRateLimiter enables per-IP rate limiting on authentication attempts.
func WithRateLimiter(rl *RateLimiter) AuthInterceptorOption {
	return func(cfg *authInterceptorConfig) {
		cfg.rateLimiter = rl
	}
}

// NewAuthInterceptor creates a Connect-RPC interceptor that validates session
// tokens and API keys from the Authorization header or ncd_session cookie.
// It applies to unary and server-streaming/bidi handler RPCs.
func NewAuthInterceptor(signer *SessionSigner, store db.Store, opts ...AuthInterceptorOption) connect.Interceptor {
	cfg := &authInterceptorConfig{}
	for _, opt := range opts {
		opt(cfg)
	}
	return authInterceptor{signer: signer, store: store, cfg: cfg}
}

type authInterceptor struct {
	signer *SessionSigner
	store  db.Store
	cfg    *authInterceptorConfig
}

func (a authInterceptor) WrapUnary(next connect.UnaryFunc) connect.UnaryFunc {
	return func(ctx context.Context, req connect.AnyRequest) (connect.AnyResponse, error) {
		authCtx, err := a.authenticateRequest(ctx, req.Spec().Procedure, req.Header(), peerFromHeader(req.Header()))
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		return next(authCtx, req)
	}
}

func (a authInterceptor) WrapStreamingClient(next connect.StreamingClientFunc) connect.StreamingClientFunc {
	return next
}

func (a authInterceptor) WrapStreamingHandler(next connect.StreamingHandlerFunc) connect.StreamingHandlerFunc {
	return func(ctx context.Context, conn connect.StreamingHandlerConn) error {
		authCtx, err := a.authenticateRequest(ctx, conn.Spec().Procedure, conn.RequestHeader(), peerFromHeader(conn.RequestHeader()))
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		return next(authCtx, conn)
	}
}

func (a authInterceptor) authenticateRequest(ctx context.Context, procedure string, header http.Header, peerAddr string) (context.Context, error) {
	// Skip auth for public procedures.

func (s *SQLiteStore) ListUsers(ctx context.Context, pageSize int, pageToken string) ([]*User, string, error) {
	if pageSize <= 0 || pageSize > 100 {
		pageSize = 50
	}

	var (
		rows *sql.Rows
		err  error
	)
	if pageToken == "" {
		rows, err = s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
			`SELECT id, email, google_sub, name, picture_url, COALESCE(stripe_id,''),
				created_at, COALESCE(suspended,0), COALESCE(suspend_reason,''), COALESCE(free_tier_used_seconds,0)
			FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?`, pageSize+1)
	} else {
		var tokenCreatedAt string
		if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT created_at FROM users WHERE id = ?`, pageToken).Scan(&tokenCreatedAt); err != nil {
			if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
				return nil, "", nil
			}
			return nil, "", err
		}
		rows, err = s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
			`SELECT id, email, google_sub, name, picture_url, COALESCE(stripe_id,''),
				created_at, COALESCE(suspended,0), COALESCE(suspend_reason,''), COALESCE(free_tier_used_seconds,0)
			FROM users WHERE created_at < ? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?`, tokenCreatedAt, pageSize+1)
	}
	if err != nil {
		return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("list users: %w", err)
	}
	defer rows.Close()

	var users []*User
	for rows.Next() {
		var (
			u         User
			createdAt string
			suspended int
		)
		if err := rows.Scan(&u.ID, &u.Email, &u.GoogleSub, &u.Name, &u.PictureURL,
			&u.StripeCustomerID, &createdAt, &suspended, &u.SuspendReason, &u.FreeTierUsedSeconds); err != nil {
			return nil, "", err
		}
		t, err := parseSQLiteTS(createdAt)
		if err != nil {
			return nil, "", err
		}
		u.CreatedAt = t
		u.Suspended = suspended != 0
		users = append(users, &u)
	}
	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
		return nil, "", err
	}

	var nextToken string

	if pageToken == "" {
		rows, err = s.pool.Query(ctx,
			`SELECT id, email, google_sub, name, picture_url, COALESCE(stripe_id, ''),
				created_at, suspended, COALESCE(suspend_reason, ''), free_tier_used_seconds
			FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $1`, pageSize+1)
	} else {
		rows, err = s.pool.Query(ctx,
			`SELECT id, email, google_sub, name, picture_url, COALESCE(stripe_id, ''),
				created_at, suspended, COALESCE(suspend_reason, ''), free_tier_used_seconds
			FROM users WHERE created_at < (SELECT created_at FROM users WHERE id = $1)
			ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2`, pageToken, pageSize+1)
	}
	if err != nil {
		return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("list users: %w", err)
	}
	defer rows.Close()

	var users []*User
	for rows.Next() {
		var u User
		if err := rows.Scan(&u.ID, &u.Email, &u.GoogleSub, &u.Name, &u.PictureURL,
			&u.StripeCustomerID, &u.CreatedAt, &u.Suspended, &u.SuspendReason,
			&u.FreeTierUsedSeconds); err != nil {
			return nil, "", err
		}
		users = append(users, &u)
	}
	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
		return nil, "", err
	}

	var nextToken string
	if len(users) > pageSize {
		nextToken = users[pageSize].ID
		users = users[:pageSize]
	}
	return users, nextToken, nil
}

func (s *PostgresStore) UpdateUserName(ctx context.Context, userID, name string) error {
	tag, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, `UPDATE users SET name = $1 WHERE id = $2`, name, userID)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("update user name: %w", err)
	}
	if tag.RowsAffected() == 0 {
		return fmt.Errorf("user not found")
	}
	return nil
}

// SetUserSuspended sets or clears the suspended flag and reason for a user.

)

type stubBillingService struct {
	t *testing.T

	createCheckoutSessionCalls []createCheckoutSessionCall
	createPortalSessionCalls   []createPortalSessionCall

	checkoutURL string
	portalURL   string
}

type createCheckoutSessionCall struct {
	customerID string
	successURL string
	cancelURL  string
	priceID    string
}

type createPortalSessionCall struct {
	customerID string
	returnURL  string
}

func (s *stubBillingService) CreateCustomer(email, name string) (string, error) {
	s.t.Helper()
	s.t.Fatalf("unexpected CreateCustomer call: %s %s", email, name)
	return "", nil
}

func (s *stubBillingService) CreateCheckoutSession(customerID, successURL, cancelURL, priceID string) (string, error) {
	s.createCheckoutSessionCalls = append(s.createCheckoutSessionCalls, createCheckoutSessionCall{
		customerID: customerID,
		successURL: successURL,
		cancelURL:  cancelURL,
		priceID:    priceID,
	})
	return s.checkoutURL, nil
}

func (s *stubBillingService) CreatePortalSession(customerID, returnURL string) (string, error) {
	s.createPortalSessionCalls = append(s.createPortalSessionCalls, createPortalSessionCall{
		customerID: customerID,
		returnURL:  returnURL,
	})
	return s.portalURL, nil
}

func (s *stubBillingService) GetCustomer(id string) (*stripe.Customer, error) {
	s.t.Helper()
	s.t.Fatalf("unexpected GetCustomer call: %s", id)
	return nil, nil
}

func (s *stubBillingService) ListPaymentMethods(customerID string) ([]*stripe.PaymentMethod, error) {
	s.t.Helper()
	s.t.Fatalf("unexpected ListPaymentMethods call: %s", customerID)
	return nil, nil
}

func testBillingAuthContext(t *testing.T, customerID string) context.Context {
	t.Helper()
	return auth.ContextWithUser(context.Background(), &db.User{

	t.Parallel()

	srv := &nanoclawServer{}

	_, err := srv.CreateCheckoutSession(context.Background(), connect.NewRequest(&ncdv1.CreateCheckoutSessionRequest{}))
	if err == nil {
		t.Fatal("expected error")
	}
	if connect.CodeOf(err) != connect.CodeUnauthenticated {
		t.Fatalf("code = %v, want %v", connect.CodeOf(err), connect.CodeUnauthenticated)
	}
}

	t.Parallel()

	srv := &nanoclawServer{}

	_, err := srv.CreateCheckoutSession(testBillingAuthContext(t, "cus_test"), connect.NewRequest(&ncdv1.CreateCheckoutSessionRequest{}))
	if err == nil {
		t.Fatal("expected error")
	}
	if connect.CodeOf(err) != connect.CodeUnavailable {
		t.Fatalf("code = %v, want %v", connect.CodeOf(err), connect.CodeUnavailable)
	}
}

func TestCreateCheckoutSessionUsesDefaultPrice(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	billing := &stubBillingService{
		t:           t,
		checkoutURL: "https://checkout.example/session",
	}
	srv := &nanoclawServer{
		billing:            billing,
		stripeDefaultPrice: "price_default",
	}

	_, err := srv.CreateCheckoutSession(testBillingAuthContext(t, "cus_test"), connect.NewRequest(&ncdv1.CreateCheckoutSessionRequest{}))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("CreateCheckoutSession: %v", err)
	}
	if len(billing.createCheckoutSessionCalls) != 1 {
		t.Fatalf("checkout call count = %d, want 1", len(billing.createCheckoutSessionCalls))
	}
	call := billing.createCheckoutSessionCalls[0]
	if call.priceID != "price_default" {
		t.Fatalf("price id = %q, want %q", call.priceID, "price_default")
	}
}

Command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,180p' cmd/ncd/account_update_test.go"
Chunk ID: e99710

Output:
package main

import (
	"context"
	"testing"

	"connectrpc.com/connect"
	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/auth"
	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
	ncdv1 "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/v1"
)

func TestUpdateAccount(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	ctx := context.Background()
	store, err := db.NewStore(ctx, "sqlite://:memory:")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("new store: %v", err)
	}
	t.Cleanup(store.Close)

	user, _, err := store.UpsertUserByExternalSubject(ctx, "google", "sub-1", "u@example.com", "Old Name", "")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("upsert user: %v", err)
	}

	srv := &nanoclawServer{store: store}
	authCtx := auth.ContextWithUser(ctx, user)

	resp, err := srv.UpdateAccount(authCtx, connect.NewRequest(&ncdv1.UpdateAccountRequest{Name: "  New Name  "}))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("UpdateAccount: %v", err)
	}
	if resp.Msg.GetAccount().GetName() != "New Name" {
		t.Fatalf("account name = %q, want %q", resp.Msg.GetAccount().GetName(), "New Name")
	}

	updated, err := store.GetUser(ctx, user.ID)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("GetUser: %v", err)
	}
	if updated.Name != "New Name" {
		t.Fatalf("stored name = %q, want %q", updated.Name, "New Name")
	}
}

func TestUpdateAccountRejectsEmptyName(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	ctx := context.Background()
	store, err := db.NewStore(ctx, "sqlite://:memory:")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("new store: %v", err)
	}
	t.Cleanup(store.Close)
	user, _, err := store.UpsertUserByExternalSubject(ctx, "google", "sub-2", "u2@example.com", "Name", "")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("upsert user: %v", err)
	}
	srv := &nanoclawServer{store: store}
	authCtx := auth.ContextWithUser(ctx, user)
	_, err = srv.UpdateAccount(authCtx, connect.NewRequest(&ncdv1.UpdateAccountRequest{Name: "   "}))
	if err == nil {
		t.Fatal("expected error")
	}
	if connect.CodeOf(err) != connect.CodeInvalidArgument {
		t.Fatalf("code = %v, want %v", connect.CodeOf(err), connect.CodeInvalidArgument)
	}
}
```

// SetStripeCustomerID sets the Stripe customer ID for a user.
func (s *PostgresStore) SetStripeCustomerID(ctx context.Context, userID, stripeID string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
		`UPDATE users SET stripe_id = $1 WHERE id = $2`, stripeID, userID)
	return err
}

// CreateSession inserts a new session record.
func (s *PostgresStore) CreateSession(ctx context.Context, sess *Session) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
		`INSERT INTO sessions (id, token_hash, user_id, created_at, expires_at, ip_address, user_agent)
		VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)`,
		sess.ID, sess.TokenHash, sess.UserID, sess.CreatedAt, sess.ExpiresAt,
		sess.IPAddress, sess.UserAgent)
	return err
}

// GetSessionByTokenHash looks up a session by its token hash.
func (s *PostgresStore) GetSessionByTokenHash(ctx context.Context, tokenHash string) (*Session, error) {
	var sess Session
	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
		`SELECT id, token_hash, user_id, created_at, expires_at,
			COALESCE(last_used, created_at), COALESCE(ip_address, ''), COALESCE(user_agent, '')
		FROM sessions WHERE token_hash = $1 AND expires_at > now()`, tokenHash,
	).Scan(&sess.ID, &sess.TokenHash, &sess.UserID, &sess.CreatedAt,
		&sess.ExpiresAt, &sess.LastUsed, &sess.IPAddress, &sess.UserAgent)
	if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session not found or expired")
	}
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get session: %w", err)
	}
	return &sess, nil
}

// TouchSession updates the last_used timestamp.
func (s *PostgresStore) TouchSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
		`UPDATE sessions SET last_used = now() WHERE id = $1`, sessionID)
	return err
}

// DeleteSession removes a session.
func (s *PostgresStore) DeleteSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = $1`, sessionID)
	return err
}

// DeleteSessionByTokenHash removes a session by its token hash.
func (s *PostgresStore) DeleteSessionByTokenHash(ctx context.Context, tokenHash string) error {
	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash)
	return err
}

// ListSessionsByUser returns all active sessions for a user.
func (s *PostgresStore) ListSessionsByUser(ctx context.Context, userID string) ([]*Session, error) {
	rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
		`SELECT id, token_hash, user_id, created_at, expires_at,
			COALESCE(last_used, created_at), COALESCE(ip_address, ''), COALESCE(user_agent, '')
		FROM sessions WHERE user_id = $1 AND expires_at > now()
		ORDER BY created_at DESC`, userID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	defer rows.Close()

	var sessions []*Session
	for rows.Next() {
```

## Assistant (commentary)

> The schema patch is in. I’m checking one detail before moving on: SQLite migration behavior, because `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN` needs to be safe both for fresh databases and already-created ones.

## Assistant

### Tool: `Bash`

```sh
rg -n "for _, stmt := range stmts|duplicate column|ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN" cmd/ncd/db/sqlite.go
```

	)`,
		`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_funding_sessions_payer ON funding_sessions(payer_kind, payer_id, created_at DESC)`,

		`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO watcher_state(id, chain_id, last_block) VALUES (1, 8453, 0)`,
	}
	for i, stmt := range stmts {
		if _, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, stmt); err != nil {
			// ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN fails with "duplicate column name" when the
			// column already exists (e.g. added to CREATE TABLE in a later migration).
			// Ignore that specific error so the migration is idempotent.
			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "duplicate column name") {
				continue
			}
			return fmt.Errorf("sqlite migration %d: %w", i, err)
		}
	}
	return nil
}

	//
	// For `collection_method=charge_automatically` a subscription moves into `incomplete` if the initial payment attempt fails. A subscription in this status can only have metadata and default_source updated. Once the first invoice is paid, the subscription moves into an `active` status. If the first invoice is not paid within 23 hours, the subscription transitions to `incomplete_expired`. This is a terminal status, the open invoice will be voided and no further invoices will be generated.
	//
	// A subscription that is currently in a trial period is `trialing` and moves to `active` when the trial period is over.
	//
	// A subscription can only enter a `paused` status [when a trial ends without a payment method](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/subscriptions/trials#create-free-trials-without-payment). A `paused` subscription doesn't generate invoices and can be resumed after your customer adds their payment method. The `paused` status is different from [pausing collection](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/subscriptions/pause-payment), which still generates invoices and leaves the subscription's status unchanged.
	//
	// If subscription `collection_method=charge_automatically`, it becomes `past_due` when payment is required but cannot be paid (due to failed payment or awaiting additional user actions). Once Stripe has exhausted all payment retry attempts, the subscription will become `canceled` or `unpaid` (depending on your subscriptions settings).
	//
	// If subscription `collection_method=send_invoice` it becomes `past_due` when its invoice is not paid by the due date, and `canceled` or `unpaid` if it is still not paid by an additional deadline after that. Note that when a subscription has a status of `unpaid`, no subsequent invoices will be attempted (invoices will be created, but then immediately automatically closed). After receiving updated payment information from a customer, you may choose to reopen and pay their closed invoices.
	Status SubscriptionStatus `json:"status"`

	TransferData *SubscriptionTransferData `json:"transfer_data"`
	// If the subscription has a trial, the end of that trial.
	TrialEnd int64 `json:"trial_end"`

struct {\n+\tCustomer      stripeRef `json:\"customer\"`\n+\tSubscription  stripeRef `json:\"subscription\"`\n+\tPaymentStatus string    `json:\"payment_status\"`\n+}\n+\n+type invoiceEvent struct {\n+\tCustomer     stripeRef `json:\"customer\"`\n+\tSubscription stripeRef `json:\"subscription\"`\n+\tAmountPaid   int64     `json:\"amount_paid\"`\n+\tPeriodEnd    int64     `json:\"period_end\"`\n+}\n+\n+type subscriptionEvent struct {\n+\tID               string    `json:\"id\"`\n+\tCustomer         stripeRef `json:\"customer\"`\n+\tStatus           string    `json:\"status\"`\n+\tCurrentPeriodEnd int64     `json:\"current_period_end\"`\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"
}

```text
Command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' cmd/ncd/billing/webhook.go"
Chunk ID: 57d5d8

Original token count: 1814
Output:
package billing

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82"
	"github.com/stripe/stripe-go/v82/webhook"

	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db"
	"github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/internal/httpx"
)

// WebhookHandler processes Stripe webhook events.
type WebhookHandler struct {
	secret string
	store  db.Store
}

// NewWebhookHandler creates a webhook handler with the given signing secret.
func NewWebhookHandler(secret string, store db.Store) *WebhookHandler {
	return &WebhookHandler{secret: secret, store: store}
}

// ServeHTTP handles incoming Stripe webhook requests.
func (h *WebhookHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
		httpx.WriteMethodNotAllowed(w, r, http.MethodPost)
		return
	}

	body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r.Body, 1<<16))
	if err != nil {
		httpx.WriteBadRequest(w, r, "webhook_read_body_failed", "invalid request body", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	event, err := webhook.ConstructEvent(body, r.Header.Get("Stripe-Signature"), h.secret)
	if err != nil {
		httpx.WriteBadRequest(w, r, "webhook_invalid_signature", "invalid signature", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	if err := h.handleEvent(r, &event); err != nil {
		slog.Error("webhook event processing failed", "event_type", event.Type, "error", err)
		httpx.WriteInternal(w, r, "webhook_processing_failed", "processing error", httpx.WithSurface("billing-webhook"), httpx.WithPathTemplate("/webhooks/stripe"))
		return
	}

	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleEvent(r *http.Request, event *stripe.Event) error {
	switch event.Type {
	case "checkout.session.completed":
		return h.handleCheckoutCompleted(r, event)
	case "invoice.paid":
		return h.handleInvoicePaid(event)
	case "invoice.payment_failed":
		return h.handleInvoicePaymentFailed(event)
	case "customer.subscription.updated":
		return h.handleSubscriptionUpdated(event)
	case "customer.subscription.deleted":
		return h.handleSubscriptionDeleted(event)
	default:
		slog.Warn("unhandled webhook event type", "event_type", event.Type)
		return nil
	}
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleCheckoutCompleted(r *http.Request, event *stripe.Event) error {
	var sess checkoutSessionEvent
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &sess); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal checkout session: %w", err)
	}
	status := ""
	if sess.Subscription.ID != "" && strings.EqualFold(sess.PaymentStatus, "paid") {
		status = "active"
	}
	if err := h.syncSubscriptionState(sess.Customer.ID, sess.Subscription.ID, status, nil); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	slog.Info("checkout completed", "customer", sess.Customer.ID, "subscription", sess.Subscription.ID)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleInvoicePaid(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var inv invoiceEvent
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &inv); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal invoice: %w", err)
	}
	var periodEnd *time.Time
	if inv.PeriodEnd != 0 {
		t := time.Unix(inv.PeriodEnd, 0).UTC()
		periodEnd = &t
	}
	if err := h.syncSubscriptionState(inv.Customer.ID, inv.Subscription.ID, "active", periodEnd); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	slog.Info("invoice paid", "customer", inv.Customer.ID, "amount", inv.AmountPaid)
	return nil
}

func (h *WebhookHandler) handleInvoicePaymentFailed(event *stripe.Event) error {
	var inv invoiceEvent
	if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data.Raw, &inv); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("unmarshal invoice: %w", err)
	}
	if err := h.syncSubscriptionState(inv.Customer.ID, inv.Subscription.ID, "past_due", nil); err != nil {
		return err
	}

[truncated]
```

        capture_pageview: false,
        enable_heatmaps: false,
        disable_session_recording: true,
        capture_exceptions: true,
        persistence: "localStorage",
      });
      posthog.capture("$pageview", {
        $current_url: window.location.href,
        referrer: document.referrer,
        tier: "anonymous",
      });
    })
    .catch(function() {});
})();
</script>

## Assistant (final_answer)

> The Stripe billing flow is now wired through the missing subscription lifecycle state.
>
> `StripeCustomerID` is no longer treated as “pro by default.” The user record now persists `stripe_subscription_id`, `stripe_subscription_status`, and `stripe_current_period_end` in both SQLite and Postgres via [store.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db/store.go), [sqlite.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db/sqlite.go), [postgres.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db/postgres.go), [migrations.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db/migrations.go), and [tracing.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/db/tracing.go). The webhook handler in [webhook.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/webhook.go) now reconciles `checkout.session.completed`, `invoice.paid`, `invoice.payment_failed`, `customer.subscription.updated`, and `customer.subscription.deleted` into that stored state. Tiering in [pricing_tier.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/pricing_tier.go) now grants `pro` only for subscription statuses that should still have access, and [serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go) returns real subscription status/id/period-end instead of hardcoding `"active"`.
>
> The transition coverage is in place. [webhook_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/billing/webhook_test.go) now exercises checkout completion, trialing, payment failure to `past_due`, invoice recovery back to `active`, and deletion to `canceled`. [serve_billing_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve_billing_test.go) covers the subscription RPC shape, and [serve_test.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve_test.go) was updated so pro-only resource gating matches the new model. I also fixed a separate HTTP error-leak regression in the demo billing handlers in [serve.go](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/serve.go) so the full `cmd/ncd/...` suite passes.
>
> Verification:
> - `go test ./cmd/ncd/...`
> - `npm --prefix cmd/ncd/ui run build`
> - `NCD_CDP_LIVE_STRIPE=1 ... go test -v -tags cdp -run 'TestCDP_AccountBillingHostedRedirects$' ./cmd/ncd -cdp-url http://127.0.0.1:8090`
>
> That leaves one practical gap: the live browser check proves hosted Stripe checkout and portal redirects still work, while the new webhook/state tests prove the subscription transitions. If you want the next slice, I’d wire a small UI surface to show the real subscription status and period end from `GetSubscription`, so the transition state is visible in the account screen rather than only enforced in backend logic.

import { h, Fragment } from "preact";
import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "preact/hooks";
import { ncdClient, accountClient } from "./client";

import { formatTimestamp } from "./util";
import type { Session, APIKeyInfo, PaymentMethod } from "./gen/ncd/v1/ncd_pb.js";
import type { DeletionStatus } from "./gen/ncd/account/v1/account_pb.js";
import { DeletionState } from "./gen/ncd/account/v1/account_pb.js";
import { notifyError, notifySuccess } from "./toast";
import { analytics, getSessionRecordingPreference, setSessionRecordingPreference } from "./analytics";
import { readHTTPErrorMessage } from "./http";

interface LinkedIdentity {
    provider: string;
    provider_sub: string;
    email: string;
    linked_at: string;
    last_sign_in_at: string;
}

interface PasskeyInfo {
    credential_id: string;
    friendly_name: string;
    created_at: string;
    last_used: string;
}

interface CheckoutSessionCreator {
    (request: {
        priceId: string;
        successUrl: string;
        cancelUrl: string;
    }): Promise<{ checkoutUrl: string }>;
}

interface BillingPortalSessionCreator {
    (request: {
        returnUrl: string;
    }): Promise<{ portalUrl: string }>;
}

interface CheckoutActionDeps {
    origin: string;
    createCheckoutSession: CheckoutSessionCreator;
    setBusy: (busy: boolean) => void;
    redirect: (url: string) => void;
    notifyError: (message: string) => void;
}

interface BillingPortalActionDeps {
    origin: string;
    createBillingPortalSession: BillingPortalSessionCreator;
    setBusy: (busy: boolean) => void;
    redirect: (url: string) => void;
    notifyError: (message: string) => void;
}

function accountBillingReturnUrl(origin: string): string {
    return origin + "/app/account";
}

export async function startCheckoutAction(deps: CheckoutActionDeps): Promise<void> {
    try {
        deps.setBusy(true);
        const res = await deps.createCheckoutSession({
            priceId: "",
            successUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
            cancelUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
        });
        deps.redirect(res.checkoutUrl);
    } catch (err: any) {
        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
        deps.notifyError("Checkout unavailable: " + message);
        deps.setBusy(false);
    }
}

export async function openBillingPortalAction(deps: BillingPortalActionDeps): Promise<void> {
    try {
        deps.setBusy(true);
        const res = await deps.createBillingPortalSession({
            returnUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),
        });
        deps.redirect(res.portalUrl);
    } catch (err: any) {
        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
        deps.notifyError("Billing portal unavailable: " + message);
        deps.setBusy(false);
    }
}

export function AccountView() {
    const auth = getAuthState();
    const [sessions, setSessions] = useState<Session[]>([]);
    const [apiKeys, setApiKeys] = useState<APIKeyInfo[]>([]);
    const [paymentMethods, setPaymentMethods] = useState<PaymentMethod[]>([]);

    const [newAPIKey, setNewAPIKey] = useState<string>("");
    const [identities, setIdentities] = useState<LinkedIdentity[]>([]);
    const [passkeys, setPasskeys] = useState<PasskeyInfo[]>([]);
    const [registeringPasskey, setRegisteringPasskey] = useState(false);
    const [deletionStatus, setDeletionStatus] = useState<DeletionStatus | null>(null);

import { AgentInstanceState, AgentTier, AgentHealthState, MigrationState, MigrationStrategy } from "./gen/ncd/agent/v1/agent_pb.js";
import { HostState } from "./gen/ncd/fleet/v1/fleet_pb.js";
import { IPAllocationState } from "./gen/ncd/network/v1/network_pb.js";
import { TerminalSessionState } from "./gen/ncd/terminal/v1/terminal_pb.js";
import type { Timestamp } from "@bufbuild/protobuf/wkt";
import { timestampDate } from "@bufbuild/protobuf/wkt";

export function formatDuration(ms: number | bigint | undefined): string {
    if (!ms) return "\u2014";
    const s = Math.floor(Number(ms) / 1000);
    if (s < 60) return `${s}s`;
    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);
    if (m < 60) return `${m}m ${s % 60}s`;
    const h = Math.floor(m / 60);
    if (h < 24) return `${h}h ${m % 60}m`;
    const d = Math.floor(h / 24);
    return `${d}d ${h % 24}h`;
}

export function formatBytes(bytes: number | bigint | undefined): string {
    if (!bytes) return "\u2014";
    const n = Number(bytes);
    if (n < 1024) return `${n} B`;
    if (n < 1048576) return `${(n / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`;
    if (n < 1073741824) return `${(n / 1048576).toFixed(1)} MB`;
    return `${(n / 1073741824).toFixed(2)} GB`;
}

export function formatTimestamp(ts: Timestamp | undefined): string {
    if (!ts) return "\u2014";
    const d = timestampDate(ts);
    return d.toLocaleString(undefined, {
        month: "short", day: "numeric",
        hour: "2-digit", minute: "2-digit", second: "2-digit",
    });
}

const AGENT_STATE_BADGES: Record<number, string> = {
    [AgentInstanceState.RUNNING]: "badge-green",
    [AgentInstanceState.IDLE]: "badge-cyan",
    [AgentInstanceState.STARTING]: "badge-yellow",
    [AgentInstanceState.SCHEDULING]: "badge-yellow",
    [AgentInstanceState.SNAPSHOTTING]: "badge-magenta",
    [AgentInstanceState.MIGRATING]: "badge-magenta",
    [AgentInstanceState.DRAINING]: "badge-yellow",
    [AgentInstanceState.STOPPED]: "badge",
    [AgentInstanceState.ERROR]: "badge-danger",
    [AgentInstanceState.TERMINATED]: "badge",
};

export function agentStateBadge(state: AgentInstanceState): string {
    return AGENT_STATE_BADGES[state] || "badge";
}

const AGENT_STATE_LABELS: Record<number, string> = {
    [AgentInstanceState.UNSPECIFIED]: "unknown",
    [AgentInstanceState.SCHEDULING]: "scheduling",
    [AgentInstanceState.STARTING]: "starting",
    [AgentInstanceState.RUNNING]: "running",
    [AgentInstanceState.IDLE]: "idle",
    [AgentInstanceState.SNAPSHOTTING]: "snapshotting",
    [AgentInstanceState.MIGRATING]: "migrating",
    [AgentInstanceState.DRAINING]: "draining",
    [AgentInstanceState.STOPPED]: "stopped",
    [AgentInstanceState.ERROR]: "error",
    [AgentInstanceState.TERMINATED]: "terminated",
};

export function agentStateLabel(state: AgentInstanceState): string {
    return AGENT_STATE_LABELS[state] || "unknown";
}

const PROFILE_LABELS: Record<number, string> = {
    [AgentTier.UNSPECIFIED]: "unknown",
    [AgentTier.INTERN]: "baseline",
    [AgentTier.SPECIALIST]: "advanced",
    [AgentTier.LEAD]: "orchestrator",
};

// Compatibility: enum is still AgentTier in the API contract.
export function tierLabel(tier: AgentTier): string {
    return PROFILE_LABELS[tier] || "unknown";
}

const HOST_STATE_BADGES: Record<number, string> = {
    [HostState.READY]: "badge-green",
    [HostState.PROVISIONING]: "badge-yellow",
    [HostState.STARTING]: "badge-yellow",
    [HostState.DRAINING]: "badge-magenta",
    [HostState.UNHEALTHY]: "badge-danger",
    [HostState.TERMINATED]: "badge",
};

export function hostStateBadge(state: HostState): string {
    return HOST_STATE_BADGES[state] || "badge";
}

const HOST_STATE_LABELS: Record<number, string> = {
    [HostState.UNSPECIFIED]: "unknown",
    [HostState.PROVISIONING]: "provisioning",
    [HostState.STARTING]: "starting",

// Connect error codes that correspond to HTTP 5xx responses.
const serverErrorCodes = new Set([
    Code.Internal,       // 500
    Code.Unavailable,    // 503
    Code.Unknown,        // 500
    Code.DataLoss,       // 500
]);

// Map Connect codes to approximate HTTP status for PostHog reporting.
function codeToStatus(code: Code): number {
    switch (code) {
        case Code.Unavailable: return 503;
        case Code.Internal:    return 500;
        case Code.DataLoss:    return 500;
        default:               return 500;
    }
}

function currentSurfaceIDFromPath(pathname: string): string {
    const head = pathname.replace(/^\/+/, "").split("/")[0];
    switch (head) {
        case "consumer":
            return "consumer";
        case "ops":
            return "ops";
        case "work":
            return "work";
        case "family":
            return "family";
        default:
            return "app";
    }
}

const surfaceContextInterceptor: Interceptor = (next) => async (req) => {
    req.header.set("x-ncd-surface-id", currentSurfaceIDFromPath(window.location.pathname));
    return next(req);
};

const serverErrorInterceptor: Interceptor = (next) => async (req) => {
    try {
        return await next(req);
    } catch (err) {
        if (err instanceof ConnectError && serverErrorCodes.has(err.code)) {
            const url = req.url;
            captureServerError(codeToStatus(err.code), url, req.method.name);

[truncated]

//go:build cdp

// CDP browser tests for the ncd web UI.
//
// These tests require a real Chrome/Chromium browser and a running ncd server.
// They are gated behind the "cdp" build tag to keep CI fast; run them with:
//
//	go test -v -tags cdp -run TestCDP ./cmd/ncd/ -cdp-url http://localhost:8090
//
// The server must be started with --dev --demo so that the dev-login bypass
// and in-memory demo state are available:
//
//	go run . serve --dev --demo --port 8090
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"image"
	_ "image/jpeg"
	_ "image/png"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/cookiejar"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"connectrpc.com/connect"
	"github.com/chromedp/chromedp"
	ncdadminv1 "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/admin/v1"
	ncdadminv1connect "github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/gen/ncd/admin/v1/ncdadminv1connect"
)

// cdpURL is the base URL of a running ncd server. Override with -cdp-url.
var cdpURL = flag.String("cdp-url", "http://localhost:8090", "ncd server base URL for CDP tests")

// cdpHeadless controls whether Chrome runs headless. Set -cdp-headless=false to watch.
var cdpHeadless = flag.Bool("cdp-headless", true, "run Chrome headless")

// newCDPCtx creates a browser context with a reasonable timeout and returns
// the context and a cancel function. Each test gets its own isolated browser
// context (tab) so tests are fully parallel-safe.
func newCDPCtx(t *testing.T, timeout time.Duration) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {
	t.Helper()

	opts := chromedp.DefaultExecAllocatorOptions[:]
	if exec := browserExecPath(); exec != "" {
		opts = append(opts, chromedp.ExecPath(exec))
	}
	if !*cdpHeadless {
		opts = append(opts, chromedp.Flag("headless", false))
	}

	allocCtx, allocCancel := chromedp.NewExecAllocator(context.Background(), opts...)
	t.Cleanup(allocCancel)

	browserCtx, browserCancel := chromedp.NewContext(allocCtx)

	ctx, timeoutCancel := context.WithTimeout(browserCtx, timeout)
	cancel := func() {
		timeoutCancel()
		browserCancel()
	}
	t.Cleanup(cancel)
	return ctx, cancel
}

// devLogin navigates to the dev login endpoint and waits for the app shell to
// appear. All tests that require authentication call this first.
func devLogin(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T) {
	t.Helper()
	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/auth/dev/login"),
		// After dev login the server redirects to the SPA.
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/app"),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(`nav, [data-testid="nav"]`, chromedp.ByQuery),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("dev login: %v", err)
	}
}

// navTo navigates to a path under cdpURL and waits for a CSS selector.
func navTo(ctx context.Context, path, waitSel string) chromedp.Action {
	return chromedp.Tasks{
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL + path),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(waitSel, chromedp.ByQuery),
	}
}

// clickAndWait clicks a selector, then waits for another to appear.
func clickAndWait(clickSel, waitSel string) chromedp.Action {
	return chromedp.Tasks{
		chromedp.Click(clickSel, chromedp.ByQuery),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(waitSel, chromedp.ByQuery),
	}
}

// getText returns the trimmed inner text of a selector.
func getText(ctx context.Context, sel string) (string, error) {
	var text string
	err := chromedp.Run(ctx, chromedp.Text(sel, &text, chromedp.ByQuery))
	return strings.TrimSpace(text), err
}

func requireLiveStripeForCDP(t *testing.T) {
	t.Helper()
	if os.Getenv("NCD_CDP_LIVE_STRIPE") == "" {
		t.Skip("set NCD_CDP_LIVE_STRIPE=1 to run live Stripe redirect checks")
	}
	if strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("STRIPE_SECRET_KEY")) == "" {
		t.Skip("STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is required for live Stripe redirect checks")
	}
	if strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("STRIPE_PRICE_ID")) == "" {
		t.Skip("STRIPE_PRICE_ID is required for live Stripe redirect checks")
	}
}

func intMax(a, b int) int {

// --------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Account management workflows
// --------------------------------------------------------------------------

// TestCDP_AccountPage verifies the account settings page loads with key
// sections: profile, sessions, API keys.
func TestCDP_AccountPage(t *testing.T) {
	ctx, _ := newCDPCtx(t, 30*time.Second)
	devLogin(ctx, t)

	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		navTo(ctx, "/app/account", `[class*="account"], h1, h2, section`),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("account page: %v", err)
	}

	body, err := getText(ctx, "body")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("get body text: %v", err)
	}
	// Account page should mention at least one of these sections.
	for _, keyword := range []string{"API", "Session", "Passkey", "Profile"} {
		if strings.Contains(body, keyword) {
			return // found at least one
		}
	}
	t.Errorf("account page body does not contain expected section headers; got: %.200s", body)
}

// TestCDP_APIKeyCreateAndRevoke exercises creating an API key through the UI
// and revoking it. Requires the account page to have a "Create API Key" flow.
func TestCDP_APIKeyCreateAndRevoke(t *testing.T) {
	ctx, _ := newCDPCtx(t, 45*time.Second)
	devLogin(ctx, t)

	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/app/account"),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(`button`, chromedp.ByQuery),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("account page: %v", err)
	}

	// Find and click "Create API Key" button.
	var clicked bool
	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Evaluate(`
			(function() {
				const btns = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button'));
				const btn = btns.find(b => b.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('api key') || b.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('create key'));
				if (btn) { btn.click(); return true; }
				return false;
			})()
		`, &clicked),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("find create api key button: %v", err)
	}
	if !clicked {
		t.Skip("Create API Key button not found on account page (feature may require live DB)")
	}

	// Fill in key name and submit.
	keyName := fmt.Sprintf("cdp-test-key-%d", time.Now().UnixNano()%10000)
	var submitted bool
	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Sleep(300*time.Millisecond),
		chromedp.Evaluate(fmt.Sprintf(`
			(function() {
				const inp = document.querySelector('input[type="text"], input[name="name"]');
				if (!inp) return false;
				inp.value = %q;
				inp.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true}));
				const btn = document.querySelector('button[type="submit"], dialog button[class*="primary"]');
				if (btn) { btn.click(); return true; }
				return false;
			})()
		`, keyName), &submitted),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("submit api key form: %v", err)
	}
	if !submitted {
		t.Log("API key form submit skipped — no submit button found")
	}
}

// TestCDP_SessionList verifies that the account page renders the current
// session in the sessions section.
func TestCDP_SessionList(t *testing.T) {
	ctx, _ := newCDPCtx(t, 30*time.Second)
	devLogin(ctx, t)

	if err := chromedp.Run(ctx,
		chromedp.Navigate(*cdpURL+"/app/account"),
		chromedp.WaitVisible(`body`, chromedp.ByQuery),
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("account page load: %v", err)
	}

	body, err := getText(ctx, "body")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("body text: %v", err)
	}
	// The sessions section should mention "current" or list a session.
	if !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(body), "session") {
		t.Errorf("account page missing sessions section; body prefix: %.300s", body)
	}
}

## Assistant (final_answer)

> The account UI now shows the real subscription lifecycle state from the backend instead of only plan and payment-method count.
>
> In [AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx) I added a `getSubscription` fetch and surfaced:
> - subscription status
> - billing cycle label (`Renews ...` or `Ends ...`)
> - Stripe subscription ID
> - a manual `Refresh Subscription` action
>
> The status mapping is explicit and testable through exported helpers, and [account-billing-actions.test.mjs](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/test/account-billing-actions.test.mjs) now covers the lifecycle labels and badge mapping for `active`, `trialing`, `past_due`, `canceled`, and inactive cases.
>
> Verification:
> - `node --test cmd/ncd/ui/test/account-billing-actions.test.mjs`
> - `npm --prefix cmd/ncd/ui run build`
> - `go test -v -tags cdp -run 'TestCDP_AccountPage$' ./cmd/ncd -cdp-url http://127.0.0.1:8090`
>
> At this point the flow is consistent end to end:
> - Stripe-hosted checkout/portal redirects work
> - webhook transitions persist local subscription state
> - tiering reads that persisted state
> - the account page renders that same state
>
> The next useful slice would be to make the account page react automatically after a checkout return, either by polling `GetSubscription` briefly or by showing a short “billing state updating” banner until the webhook-driven state lands.

function accountBillingReturnUrl(origin: string): string {
    return origin + "/app/account";

export function subscriptionStatusLabel(status: string): string {
    switch (status.trim().toLowerCase()) {
        case "active":
            return "Active";
        case "trialing":
            return "Trialing";
        case "past_due":
            return "Past due";
        case "canceled":
            return "Canceled";
        case "unpaid":
            return "Unpaid";
        case "incomplete":
            return "Incomplete";
        case "incomplete_expired":
            return "Expired";
        case "paused":
            return "Paused";
        default:
            return "Inactive";
    }
}

export function subscriptionStatusBadge(status: string): string {
    switch (status.trim().toLowerCase()) {
        case "active":
        case "trialing":
            return "badge badge-green";
        case "past_due":
        case "unpaid":
        case "incomplete":
            return "badge badge-yellow";
        case "canceled":
        case "incomplete_expired":
            return "badge";
        case "paused":
            return "badge badge-magenta";
        default:
            return "badge";
    }
}

export function subscriptionRenewalLabel(subscription: Pick<GetSubscriptionResponse, "status" | "currentPeriodEnd"> | null): string {
    if (!subscription) {
        return "\u2014";
    }
    const status = subscription.status.trim().toLowerCase();
    if (!subscription.currentPeriodEnd) {
        return status === "inactive" || status === "" ? "No active renewal" : "\u2014";
    }
    const ts = formatTimestamp(subscription.currentPeriodEnd);
    if (status === "canceled") {
        return `Ends ${ts}`;
    }
    return `Renews ${ts}`;
}

            priceId: "",
            successUrl: accountBillingReturnUrl(deps.origin),

        // Re-connect when the tab becomes visible or the browser comes back online.
        function onResume() {
            if (cancelled) return;
            // Only act if we're waiting to reconnect (not mid-stream).
            if (reconnectTimer !== undefined) {
                clearTimeout(reconnectTimer);
                reconnectTimer = undefined;
                connect();
            }
        }

        document.addEventListener("visibilitychange", onResume);
        window.addEventListener("online", onResume);

        connect();

        return () => {
            cancelled = true;
            ctrl.abort();
            if (reconnectTimer !== undefined) clearTimeout(reconnectTimer);
            document.removeEventListener("visibilitychange", onResume);
            window.removeEventListener("online", onResume);
            setStatus("disconnected");
        };
    }, deps);

    return { status };
}

// Notify callback when the page becomes visible and online.
function awaitReady(cb: () => void): void {
    function check() {
        if (!document.hidden && navigator.onLine) {
            document.removeEventListener("visibilitychange", check);
            window.removeEventListener("online", check);
            cb();
        }
    }
    document.addEventListener("visibilitychange", check);
    window.addEventListener("online", check);
}

// Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, …, capped at 30s, with ±25% jitter.
function backoff(attempt: number): number {
    const base = Math.min(1000 * Math.pow(2, attempt - 1), 30_000);
    const jitter = base * 0.25 * (Math.random() * 2 - 1);
    return Math.round(base + jitter);
}

const serverErrorCodes = new Set([Code.Internal, Code.Unavailable, Code.Unknown, Code.DataLoss]);

function isServerError(code: Code): boolean {
    return serverErrorCodes.has(code);
}

function codeToHTTPStatus(code: Code): number {

## Assistant (final_answer)

> The account page now handles the post-Stripe return path more cleanly.
>
> In [AccountView.tsx](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/src/AccountView.tsx) I added a short billing-state refresh loop after load plus a visibility-triggered refresh when the tab becomes active again. While subscription/payment-method state is still converging, the page shows a small “Billing state is updating” note instead of silently showing stale data. The stop condition is explicit in `shouldKeepPollingBillingState()`, and the banner text is driven by `billingSyncMessage()` so the behavior is deterministic.
>
> I also extended [account-billing-actions.test.mjs](/Volumes/tmc/go/src/github.com/tmc/nanoclaw/cmd/ncd/ui/test/account-billing-actions.test.mjs) to cover the new polling and sync-message logic. Verification:
> - `node --test cmd/ncd/ui/test/account-billing-actions.test.mjs`
> - `npm --prefix cmd/ncd/ui run build`
> - `go test -v -tags cdp -run 'TestCDP_AccountPage$' ./cmd/ncd -cdp-url http://127.0.0.1:8090`
>
> At this point the billing flow is covered end to end:
> - hosted Stripe checkout and billing portal redirects
> - webhook-driven subscription transitions
> - persisted tier/subscription state
> - account UI rendering and short-lived post-return convergence
>
> The next logical step is to add a live webhook-driven browser test with Stripe CLI forwarding if you want the final gap closed under automation rather than targeted unit/integration coverage.
